programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A terminally ill patient begs for Dr Sloan's help. Ten years before, he was wrongly convicted of his wife's murder - now he wants to find the culprit and prove his innocence before he dies. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark takes on a temporary assignment as a physician on a cruise ship, where he finds himself investigating the killing of a passenger with more than his fair share of enemies. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Pursued by the local police, Kimble takes refuge working as a handyman for a widow and her young son, but unintentionally makes an enemy of her suitor

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: The owner of a football team is accused of murdering her husband, an alcoholic gambler who was trying to sell his share of the team for some quick cash

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker has to save CD's niece after her ex-boyfriend kidnaps her and embarks on a spree of bank robberies with the girl in tow

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Criminals kidnap a bank employee's baby to force her to provide them with inside information on how to commit a robbery - but two are killed in the ensuing raid and the third escapes without revealing the youngster's location. The team immediately mounts a search, but work is hampered by relentless rain and an unexpected terrorist response drill

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team goes in search of a stolen radiotherapy canister that could have been used to make a bomb after a man impersonating a Navy Seal dies after being set on fire. Meanwhile, team leader Hetty Lange has been acting strangely and Callen offers to help with whatever is troubling her. Guest starring Claire Forlani (Camelot), with Chris O'Donnell and Linda Hunt

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble calls on Dr McAllister, a former mentor, but does not realise that his friend is both physically and mentally ill

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: After Perry successfully defends a woman accused of murdering her husband, she claims she is being contacted by the dead man's spirit

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Col Mann arrives to assist with inquiries into the murder of a marine - a case that becomes personal for Gibbs when he learns one of the witnesses is his ex-wife Stephanie. The team discovers the prime suspect was having an affair with the dead man's spouse, but the case is complicated by the revelation that the victim may have been selling top-secret military information. Starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker and Trivette investigate when the coach of a local football team is found dead

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and the team are perplexed by Hetty's sudden resignation and press Vance and their new team leader Lauren Hunter for more information about what happened to Hetty. Visiting one of her homes for clues, they find mercenaries ransacking the place - and try to track down the man who hired them. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While performing an autopsy for a class of military students, Ducky discovers the cadaver he is using is the body of a murder victim. In trying to identify the corpse, Gibbs and his team are set on the trail of a career criminal specialising in identity fraud. Meanwhile, Ziva has difficulty separating her personal feelings and professional judgment. Starring David McCallum and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Hetty confronts Alexa Comescu and tells her Callen is dead, but the crime boss knows she is lying. Meanwhile, the agents search for their leader in Romania hoping to confirm she is alive, but when they mount a rescue attempt one of the team is shot. Real-time episode of the crime drama, starring Linda Hunt, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Seal is shot dead while threatening to throw himself from the top of a building. An al-Qaeda operative emerges as the main suspect, but DiNozzo thinks the killer may actually be found closer to home. Elsewhere, a rumour spreads that Abby may be about to leave NCIS for good. Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star in the American drama

programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey spreads her wings for the first time since her husband was put behind bars, and invites a handsome footballer round for dinner. Meanwhile, Sharon discovers the joys of literature. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Smithy and Max investigate the killing of Jacko Jackson, but the list of suspects continues to grow. Grandfather Johnnie admits to rowing with the lad and his estranged son Roger Hutton has a clear motive for wanting him out of the way, while girlfriend Anya confesses to coming to blows with him on the night of the crime. Guest starring Edward and Tim Woodward

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark is torn between maintaining his friendships and his duty to the hospital when Hanssen tells him he must drop a consultant from the rota. However, when Elliot bends the rules to help a patient again, the choice appears to have been made for him. Meanwhile, Kieran returns, desperate to win Donna's affections before he leaves for Afghanistan

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An optimistic Nina accompanies Ellen to her appointment with the specialist. Selena confides in Maggie as she becomes increasingly concerned about Nathan's preoccupation with her, and the pair sneak into his office, where they find incriminating CCTV footage. Meanwhile, Sam helps the new receptionist settle in

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jenny tells Dave about her decision to move out and make a new life for herself elsewhere, leaving him free to commit to Polly. However, as the past catches up with him, the outcome isn't as clearcut as he thought

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dan has some explaining to do after an early morning surprise for Tony and Simon. Clare decides to intervene and try to sort out Nigel's problems

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: After his row with Simon, Tony faces a difficult day. Nigel keeps his distance from Grant but Lorraine visits him and opens her heart

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Lieutenant Colonel Philip Drysdale, the new CO, arrives with his traditional views on army life and is unimpressed the attitude of B Company's men

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen practises her first-aid skills on James, and Siegfried is less than sympathetic when Tristan complains about deputising for Mrs Hall. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A flag from the American War of Independence turns up in a local church and triggers a wave of public interest as a number of villagers come forward to claim it as their own. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William's secretary calls in sick, so Hester volunteers to run the office in her place. However, when an important new client shows up for an appointment she finds herself having to fill in for her missing husband. Comedy, starring Anton Rogers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey decides that if Diana and Tom are seen to be mentally incompetent it will give him power of attorney over their affairs and end all his woes. However, the couple are wise to him, and plan a well-orchestrated display for the visiting psychiatrist. Comedy, with Graham Crowden, Stephanie Cole and Daniel Hill

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy, Clegg and an enthusiastic Compo plot to save Smiler from the terrible fate of being Nora Batty's ex-lodger

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy comes up with the crazy idea of safety underwear after he collides with Compo while riding his old bicycle. Meanwhile, Howard tries several futile methods of increasing his height after concluding Pearl is attracted to taller men

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The final episode of the detective drama sees Gently testifying against corrupt Met officers as he prepares to retire from the force. However, he is persuaded to take on one final investigation - re-opening the case of a woman who was murdered four years previously. Gently's search for the truth leads him to investigate a reforming politician who is tipped to become the next prime minister and uncover a web of political intrigue that places Bacchus and Rachel in danger. Starring Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ted leads an investigation into the apparent suicide of a city trader who was embroiled in the cut-throat world of London's financial district in the 1980s. As the team delve deeper into the macho fraternity of 'The Wolfpack', they find a darker side to the debauchery, secret hand signals and all-nighters that bonded the traders together - including a strict hierarchy that might be worth killing for. Meanwhile, Steve tries to learn some tricks of the trade, amid his own financial crisis. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. A rising star in the Essex police force is found beaten and shot in his burnt-out car in what proves to be an emotional case for his colleagues. The team helps DCI Jim Sullivan, the detective charged with bringing the killer to justice, and it isn't long before suspicion falls on Dean Fallon - a local drug dealer and the son of a known crime boss. But when he proves to have a solid alibi, the investigation shifts closer to home. Starring Emilia Fox and David Caves, with James Alexandrou and Jason Watkins

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jenny tells Dave about her decision to move out and make a new life for herself elsewhere, leaving him free to commit to Polly. However, as the past catches up with him, the outcome isn't as clearcut as he thought

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk investigates the murder of a pregnant woman and the priest who rejected her confession. At first it looks as though suicide is a major factor in the case, but Cadfael is not convinced and, of course, he is proved right once the clues start falling into place. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ellen defiantly throws the letter about her chemotherapy session in the bin. She breaks down in front of a sympathetic Nina after lying to everyone about her test results. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A man is arrested for attempted murder, but claims he only hit out with a hammer to fight off two knife-wielding intruders and protect his daughters. Emma follows a hunch he is telling the truth and sets up a ruse with fellow officers to prove his innocence. While the team celebrate the result, by-the-book Sergeant Callum Stone is clearly not happy about his junior colleague's maverick methods

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alan demands some answers from Frankie but does not like what he hears. Tony is thrilled to see Simon but worries about his night with Frankie, and Mark sets off for America

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel wants to get fit. Lorraine wants to spend more time with Grant, Gita is worried about her hospital appointment, and Pat and Roy return from honeymoon

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The arrival of strict new sergeant-major Alan Fitzpatrick causes quite a stir among the ranks, while memories of traumatic episode from Hobbs' past come flooding back during an exercise, threatening his chances of promotion. The boys persuade a reluctant Butcher to invest in a greyhound and the wives reveal their competitive side while organising a charity auction

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A local rascal goes on the straight and narrow to pay for his dog's veterinary bills, and Tristan has designs on Debbie Mount - but her father does not approve. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish dealer tries to beat Charlie Gimbert to a priceless collection of Jewish antiques that has turned up in Essex. Alfred Marks guest stars with Ian McShane

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: With Emma's wedding preparations in full swing, William and Hester prepare to meet their new in-laws. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A councillor trying to win the election by attracting the OAP vote incurs Diana's wrath. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy launches a series of nature tours after coming to believe he has discovered the nesting place of a giant woodpecker. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy is inspired by the sight of a sailboarder slicing through water, and tries to create a sailboard big enough for him and his two friends to stand on. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The Lynleys are looking forward to becoming parents, but Helen succumbs to temptation and dabbles in her husband's latest case. However, her past involvement with the victim unwittingly puts her in the firing line, with devastating consequences for everyone. Jemma Redgrave and Barbara Flynn guest star, with Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Lesley Vickerage

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: With Sasha back on fighting form, UCOS investigates the death of a talented cricket prodigy, who was the apple of his father's eye, yet loathed by his on-field team-mates. The death was recorded as misadventure but with new evidence pointing to infighting, over-zealous autograph hunters and extra marital affairs, UCOS are soon bowled over by the list of potential murder suspects. Meanwhile, wise to Steve's monetary problems, Danny appoints himself as his personal financial adviser. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a DJ leads Nikki to question her judgement in a strikingly similar case from her past and she brings the potential mistake to the attention of her colleagues, but the news threatens to have repercussions for the family of the deceased, the police and the Lyell Centre. The fresh evidence leads to the conclusion that a copycat killer is at large and Nikki finds evidence that an intruder has been entering her home. Feature-length episode of the drama, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: Feature-length drama in which the Middle Ages' answer to Sherlock Holmes sets out to investigate a slave girl's disappearance and locate a treasured relic missing from his abbey. Unfortunately, no sooner has he begun searching for a connection than a dead body turns up on his doorstep, plunging him into a mire of murder and subterfuge. Starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Anthony Green, George Irving and Louise Delamere

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's heating breaks down, leaving them with no one to turn to - except their man-hungry neighbour Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.desc: As the bills pile up, Tracey has to consider selling Darryl's car, while Sharon goes into the perfume business to raise some cash. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: In an effort to find out what life is like for Darryl behind bars, Tracey decides to build her own prison in the attic - with Sharon as her cellmate. Comedy, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.desc: A man is arrested for attempted murder, but claims he only hit out with a hammer to fight off two knife-wielding intruders and protect his daughters. programme.desc: Malick's confrontational attitude prompts a female patient to make a formal complaint against him, and he is forbidden to operate on her. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Two troublemakers fall through the roof of a hall while a charity football match takes place nearby. Referee Luke tends the boys and takes one of them under his wing when he spots his potential as a soccer player. Ellen struggles to hide her cancer, while Maggie receives a romantic e-mail from a stranger

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DC Jacob Banks (Patrick Robinson) joins the Sun Hill watch in time to help a hung-over Mickey investigate the murder of a private detective and soon establishes himself as a member of the team. Meanwhile, Callum continues to single Emma out for harsh treatment among all his PCs and Sally catches the backlash of her resentment. Gordon Kennedy and Anna Calder-Marshall guest star

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Square remembers Arthur. Phil gets into trouble at the Arches. Frankie and Alan argue

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie tells Alan about sleeping with Tony. Ricky finds the Arches closed. Phil gets drunk, then mugged. Tiffany confronts Grant and Lorraine after Bianca tells her the truth

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Personal problems are high on the troops' agenda as Andy Butcher's mother throws his love life into disarray, Angela McCleod's house is broken into and Sergeant-Major Alan Fitzpatrick learns he cannot treat his daughter like a private on parade

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's plans to celebrate his anniversary in style seem doomed when all his fellow vets want the same night off - and a goat makes matters worse by eating his cheque book. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques expert stumbles on what seems to be a bargain in Frankie's scrap yard in the shape of an iron cannon. Though convinced his luck must finally have changed, problems lie ahead when the item attracts the attention of a devious major. Tongue-in-cheek drama, with Ian McShane and guest star Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides to call in the experts to deal with redecorating - but her choice is less than ideal. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Following the death of a resident, Tom puts himself forward as a counsellor to aid and comfort the terminally ill. Meanwhile, Jane becomes confused when she overhears Tom and Diana's discussion over the existence of God. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy ropes Compo and Clegg into reconstructing a battle when a pit at a building site brings back memories of his wartime exploits. Comedy, starring Brain Wilde and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo, Clegg and Foggy are pleased when a stranger gives them an old van, but little do they realise what is inside it. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills is persuaded to act on behalf of a drug addict, who went on the run 16 years previously after being charged with attempted murder. The defendant alleges detectives forced him to sign a confession and allowed him to escape custody - leaving Deed to decide if they should be charged with perverting the course of justice. Starring Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden, Jenny Seagrove, Caroline Langrishe and Robert Cavanah

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Sasha is occupied with a training course, the boys' investigations take them to a lookalike agency, where their top two acts were found dead in an apparent double suicide 13 years ago. In an industry reliant on the art of the illusion and their suspects intent on giving them the run around, Ted, Steve and Danny are convinced there's more to this case than first meets the eye. Steve is thrilled when their enquiries take them to the door of one of his football heroes, but his glee is short-lived. Detective drama, starring Denis Lawson

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The murder of Amir Aziz, a prominent anti-fundamentalist Muslim, drags the Lyell team into the murky world of hate-crime and counter-terrorism. Initially thought to be the work of a EDL member whose son was killed by the Taliban, the team begin to suspect the killing is connected to Sarah Begovic, a young woman who left the UK to relocate to Syria, but who recently returned home in secret with her six-month-old son. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Lintern

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A woman's corpse is discovered in a ploughed field, and the only lead is her husband, a potter. A year earlier, he suddenly decided to start a new life as a monk, suggesting that his wife may have committed suicide because she could not bear to lose him to the brethren - or that her spouse may have had a hand in her death. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: James and Norman play with James' new remote control aeroplane, but after a collision with Steele, he bans them from continuing

programme.desc: This episode takes us to icy northern Russia to meet the walrus and to the desert of Egypt to meet the scorpion

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the UKIP candidate for the London mayoral election .

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Kush plans a huge surprise for Whitney, his mood is brought down when Gray reminds him that by accepting Phil's extra charges he will be going to prison for a very long time. Keegan proudly sets up his new market stall - but what is Tiffany hiding? Karen receives an unusual proposition but takes it the wrong way

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil decides to enhance the hotel's reputation with an exclusive night of gourmet cuisine. His new chef has a cordon bleu rating and loves every type of dish - but he is particularly taken with Manuel. Classic comedy, written by and starring John Cleese and Connie Booth. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the climate activist over the course of 2019 and 2020, as she travels the world exploring the science of global warming and challenging world leaders to take action on the growing crisis. The programme follows the impact of Covid-19 on her campaign, and the challenges of persuading a world living through one crisis to face up to the possibility of another on the horizon. In the first programme, Greta travels through North America on her way to a UN climate conference in Chile

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: The couple clash at home before a big family trip to New York. Back in Bolton it's 'new hair who dis' for Faryal before an emotional chat about Amir's plans for one last dance

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Amir puts a pause on training for a quick trip to Dubai for some holiday home shopping. Back in Bolton, the wedding hall is causing more headache and Amir fears the worst.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development of auction-bought properties in Merthyr Tydfil, Coventry and Lower Gornal in the West Midlands, one of which is being converted into two flats

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A convict claims he had an accident in the prison gym, a group of women stages a fall in a supermarket and when a Rolex gets ruined, the claimant has problems recalling his timings

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, ambulance crew Stephen and Gwion are called to a festival worker who needs treatment for a reaction to a bee sting. An elderly patient with labyrinthitis has become dizzy and needs to be taken to hospital. A sepsis case at a GP surgery needs emergency treatment and a fast transfer to hospital.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Newmarket Racecourse, Suffolk, where two teams of married couples go head to head, with help from experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home from South Africa, the latest potential buyer has a budget of £450,000 and heads west to Cornwall, with Steve Brown leading the search for a character property that has holiday let potential. Steve also pays a visit to the Newlyn Copper Works and has a bash at transforming the striking reddish metal into a delicate work of art

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by two best friends, one of whom likes traditional cooking while the other is more adventurous

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Liberal Democrat candidate for mayor of London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is desperate to find Kush, terrified he has run away before his court appearance. The Carters are determined to make Frankie's birthday special by throwing a party at the Vic, where an unexpected guest surprises everyone. A mistake on social media puts the spotlight on Keegan

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The outcome of his compensation case against the hospital inspires Dominic to refashion his life, but when his plans are dashed, he's soon asking a huge favour of Sacha. Kian's bromance with a troubled patient takes a surprising turn when he and Chloe make a shocking discovery in theatre, while money matters continue to mount for Max

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A 95-year-old navy veteran is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He is diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. He is also 100 miles from hospital, where the nightshift is about to get very busy. Meanwhile, the dental team takes to the road to reach patients across Shetland

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: After hours of searching Monaco, the kennel colleagues track Frank to his hotel. Now is their chance to force him to admit he is living the high life on their winnings - only for their plan to be scuppered when the security guards throw them out of the hotel. Back in Yorkshire, the remaining staff are struggling to keep the understaffed kennels running smoothly, while Colette worries that Richard and his wealthy business associates will never accept her

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Harsimrat and Jagjit celebrate the arrival of their first-born baby at home, while 15-year-old Ansh learns about the sacred meaning of his turban. Newlyweds Hari and Kiran continue their devotion to their guru through their marriage and in their daily lives, and Hardeep scatters his father's ashes over flowing water to unite him with God

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman lets Cody in on a family secret. Dylan gets caught up in Zane's conflict and Norma joins forces with a stranger in the anti-bypass campaign

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman and Norma's relationship is put in jeopardy by a growing secret and Emma finds herself in a dangerous situation. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why astronaut Tim Peake's space capsule has landed in the city's 900-year-old cathedral

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Women's Equality Party candidate for mayor of London

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Three new sellers arrive hoping to persuade the dealers to bid against each other for a child's penny farthing, two unusual optician's mirrors and an antique ice shaver

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four top chefs compete for the London and the South East region with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. One of the four contestants must leave the competition at the end of the episode, as decided by this week's veteran judge

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding reveals how a team of 'super recognisers' - police officers with an extraordinary ability to recognise faces - could be the key to catching criminals who commit multiple offences

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: The sex of a penguin can only be determined by a blood test, so the penguin keepers' annual sweepstake is launched to guess the sex of all the new youngsters. However, they've noticed that young Nessie has a spinal deformity and the vets are called in to investigate. New babies at both Edinburgh Zoo and the Highland Wildlife Park are keeping everyone busy - not just penguins, but lion cubs, caribou deer and parrots

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: The Masters 2019: Tiger Roars Again
programme.desc: A look back at Tiger Woods' memorable victory at Augusta National Golf Club, which saw the American win this tournament for the fifth time in his career, and raise his overall tally of Major victories to 15. Woods is second only to Jack Nicklaus, who triumphed six times at this famous golf course, and finished with 18 Major titles to his name

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques in Southwell Minster, near Nottingham. Experts Michael Baggott and Mark Stacey try to find the best collectibles inspired by nature, while Paul learns how the green man sculptures that cover the minster chapter house first took their inspiration from ancient pagan beliefs - which could have links with local legend Robin Hood

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Among the radical transformations are disused sideboard that is turned into a special item for a family, and an ornate clock of huge sentimental value. Sabrina Grant presents

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality redesigns for a spacious Wiltshire home that used to be a hair salon - so is poorly designed for actually living in, with a ground floor that feels like two separate properties, each with its own front door. Laura gives the building a welcoming entrance while making the interior family-friendly and functional, while Robert comes up with an innovative approach that cuts down on the amount of floor space but which could radically alter the way the owners live

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the third semi-final of the quiz, with four more returning contenders answering questions on specialist subjects of the films of Powell and Pressburger, the Bauhaus movement, Emil Zatopek, and the Liberal Democrats, before they each are teted on their general knowledge

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the fourth semi-final of the quiz, with four more returning contenders answering questions on the specialist subjects of REM, Father Ted, David Niven, and John Wesley, before they are each tested on general knowledge

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hotel in South Africa that has been built into a 187-foot tall former grain silo and which also incorporates a museum of contemporary art. They are challenged to wash the building's 96 windows inside and out, and discover how staff have coped with the challenge of keeping the place clean in the wake of the worst drought to hit Cape Town in a century

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor David Tennant hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jack Dee and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to look back at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan scours the globe for solutions to environmental problems, examining the natural and technological fixes that can help people slow the rate of decline and adapt to shifts already taking place. He begins in the Solomon Islands and then travels down the east coast of Australia from the Great Barrier Reef to Tasmania, learning about the 'feminisation' of green turtles and coming face-to-face with the terror of a bushfire

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: Paloma Faith: As I Am
programme.desc: Documentary following the singer-songwriter as she balances the demands of a make-or-break tour with writing a new album, launching an acting career and being a first-time mum. The film observes Paloma's life both front and backstage in the midst of this pivotal year for her career, and sees her pitted against a music industry that does not always look too kindly on motherhood

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Christchurch Park in Ipswich, Suffolk, where treasures include an Imperial Chinese porcelain vase fit for an emperor and some intriguing portraits related to the Profumo scandal of the 1960s. Also featured are intricate, solid gold lapel badges from the Australian gold fields and a pair of delicate paintings by a famous Suffolk artist

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper, before seeing the items auctioned by Charles Hanson. Cameras also capture a Chinese treasure discovered inside a carrier bag on its way to a charity shop go under the hammer

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It's the Easter weekend, which means extra time to enjoy the garden. Monty Don plants out sweet peas for summer colour and gives advice on what to use for mulching borders. Plus, Carol Klein is at RHS Rosemoor to revel in the floral spring sensations that epitomise the season

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After his eventful evening at Woody and Tracy's wedding, George tries once and for all to sort out his love life without upsetting anyone. Erik Thomson stars

programme.title: Nature's Great Migrations
programme.desc: Documentary following thousands of zebra as they face lions, hunting dogs, drought and starvation to undertake the world's most newly discovered migration. Braving one of Botswana's most brutal wildernesses along the way, the animals make Africa's longest land migration to reach the grasslands of Nxai Pan National Park. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists and filmmakers use cutting-edge technology to capture the zebra every step of the way, and reveal the secrets behind the brand new finding

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall turns his attention to Africa, exploring Botswana's Okavango Delta, one of the world's largest inland deltas which is home to a wide and diverse spectrum of wildlife. He asks what makes it possible for a river to stop in the middle of a desert and reveals an astonishing secret

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion head to Wrest Park in Bedfordshire to view and value a succession of antiques and collectibles. Presenter Paul Martin explores the grounds of the country estate, taking a look at its collection of statuary and finding out about the key characters who made Wrest what it is today

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A classic steamer trunk which brings back emotional childhood memories and a collection of piano accordions are among today's transformations

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a couple's home in Margate. The property was originally a B&B, and the transition to a home has not been entirely successful. It is poorly laid out, has too many rooms, and while big on floor space is lacking natural light. Laura sets out to return the building to its early 20th-century roots, whole Robert comes up with a radically different way of using the living space

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as eight talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. In each episode, the competitors are faced with two very different challenges, with their work judged by are two of the biggest names in the business, Solange Azagury-Partridge and Shaun Leane, before one contestants leaves the competition

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The presenter returns to Springfield Hospital to work with the team and meet patients in crisis once again as they battle mental health issues during the Covid-19 pandemic. She meets patients whose conditions have been exacerbated by the challenges of lockdown, including two people struggling with psychosis and someone who is trying to control the daily rituals that govern her life

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Jessica Fostekew and Stephen Bailey

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: Told through candid interviews and personal archive, cameras reveal what happens as lockdowns continue around the planet and the pressure of being trapped indoors begins to be felt. Contributors include a French photojournalist who is forced to decide between working and home-schooling, and heads to the frontline of the pandemic

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five returning contestants have to prepare a dish they would want on the menu of their own restaurant. Guest judge Anna Haugh is on hand to assess their efforts, resulting in two eliminations before the remaining chefs prepare a meal for the staff of Michelin-starred restaurant the Fat Duck, under the watchful eye of former executive chef Jonny Lake

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, where Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss guide the red and blue teams. Christina also finds out about Tudor architecture

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies assess more pitches. A pair of tea entrepreneurs find themselves in hot water when their numbers are interrogated, the Dragons discover hidden artistic talents when a mindfulness art business is pitched, an optical inventor hopes to have investment in sight with his anti-glare eyewear and a husband-and-wife team have the needs of NHS workers at the heart of their skin-care business. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Yasmin, Miki and Andrew must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to Cardiff to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into each before deciding which one they like the most. Narrated by Marcus Garve

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Katie tackle the difficult issue of taking their burgeoning relationship to the next level, while Arlo is still struggling to work things out with Lindsey and Poppy. Elsewhere, Shay is led astray by Siouxsie, and Big Mac launches a plan to entice more tourists to Weld. Erik Thomson stars

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Kate Humble, Patrick Ayree, scientists and wildlife cameramen examine the changing seasons at Yellowstone National Park during 2016, and the effect on the local American wildlife. In the first episode, Patrick follows the grizzly bears, who are taking a risk with the weather by leaving their winter dens early. Meanwhile, Kate gets to grips with the science behind this frozen season

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall concludes his look at the planet's most fascinating ecosystems with a look at the Arctic archipelago of Svalbard. He reveals that it is home to an array of wildlife including the world's largest land predator, and uncovers a surprising secret about the remote region

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Adam Partridge appraise collectibles at Chiddingstone Castle in Kent. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits Brands Hatch and experiences what's made this track a huge success for 100 years

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A grandfather clock that has been in a family for over 100 years and a drum kit with a very special connection are among the transformations by the skilled team of crafters

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a three-bed home in Surrey, which has a poorly positioned front door and a cluttered hallway. The owners are divided over whether an extension would solve their problems, and the architects present them with two very different visions for what their home could be. Laura takes a characteristically pragmatic approach with a simple but striking contemporary design. But experimental architect Robert decides a far more radical approach is the only answer

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the Wales heats sees Hywel Griffith, Chris Cleghorn, Nathan Davies and Ali Borer prepare their starters, with one chef leaving at the end of the programme. Which one that is will be decided by the veteran judge of this week's competition, a previous winner who remains a surprise to the contenders until the moment they walk into the kitchen to taste the first course

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: Documentary examining corruption in the police in the 1970s, revealing a secret network of officers was operating illegally throughout London and led to the formation of the first internal anti-corruption unit A10, which inspired the BBC drama Line of Duty. The story begins in 1969, when a desperate south London criminal tipped off the Times about how he was being extorted for money by a detective in the Metropolitan Police

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Richard Osman, Lolly Adefope and Lee Mack join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'naked truth' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The presenter returns to Springfield Hospital to work with the team and meet patients in crisis once again as they battle mental health issues during the Covid-19 pandemic. She meets patients whose conditions have been exacerbated by the challenges of lockdown, including two people struggling with psychosis and someone who is trying to control the daily rituals that govern her life

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue as the second group of surviving amateurs cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or featured in their cookbook. In an unexpected twist, the contestants discover that they are also presenting their food to top chef Marcus Wareing. After two leave the contest, the remaining three head to The Game Bird restaurant at The Stafford Hotel to work with culinary director Ben Tish, serving the restaurant's staff. No one knows the food better than they do and expectations are high of the contenders

programme.title: My Father and Me (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary maker Nick Broomfield takes a distinctly personal look at his relationship with his humanist-pacifist father Maurice, a factory worker turned photographer of vivid images of postwar England. These images inspired Nick's own film-making career, but also spoke to a difference in outlook between the pair

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Exploring more of Northern Ireland's most spectacular countryside, Barra Best investigates County Antrim's lost railway routes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a beautiful oval painting of an old cabin at the end of a weathered pathway

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a small watercolour sketch that could be the work of sculptor Henry Moore. The piece was found in 2012 in a hoard of artwork stolen by the Nazis. While the unidentified piece has many characteristics of Moore's work, none of the other artworks recovered were by British artists, so it remains a mystery how it came to be there. The team must not only find out who created it, but also who it belonged to, since it may have been looted from Jewish owners during the Holocaust and will need to be returned to surviving descendants

programme.title: An Evening with Peter Alliss
programme.desc: A celebration of the golf commentator and broadcaster's life and career, featuring anecdotes from his wife Jackie, golfers Tom Watson, Darren Clarke and Gary Player, and celebrities including Gary Lineker, Terry Wogan, Chris Evans, Bruce Forsyth, Jimmy Tarbuck, Hazel Irvine and Steve Rider. Plus, clips from some of his popular programmes, including Pro-Celebrity Golf, A Round with Alliss and A Golfer's Travels. Narrated by Sue Johnston

programme.title: Around with Alliss
programme.desc: Peter Alliss enjoys a chat and a round of golf with Terry Wogan

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the development of Impressionism, when France was changing at a rapid pace and young artists re-invented the rules of painting - finding their muses in the bars, brothels and cabarets of Paris and turning the world of art on its head. Monet, Degas and their contemporaries launched a heated debate about the role of painting in the modern world that would pave the way for just about every modern art movement of note

programme.title: The Incredible Story of Marie Antoinette's Watch with Nicholas Parsons
programme.desc: The Just a Minute host explores the life and work of Swiss horologist Abraham-Louis Breguet and goes in search of his most famous timepiece, which is valued at up to $100m

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a beautiful oval painting of an old cabin at the end of a weathered pathway

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Exploring more of Northern Ireland's most spectacular countryside, Barra Best investigates County Antrim's lost railway routes

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a small watercolour sketch that could be the work of sculptor Henry Moore. The piece was found in 2012 in a hoard of artwork stolen by the Nazis. While the unidentified piece has many characteristics of Moore's work, none of the other artworks recovered were by British artists, so it remains a mystery how it came to be there. The team must not only find out who created it, but also who it belonged to, since it may have been looted from Jewish owners during the Holocaust and will need to be returned to surviving descendants

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the development of Impressionism, when France was changing at a rapid pace and young artists re-invented the rules of painting - finding their muses in the bars, brothels and cabarets of Paris and turning the world of art on its head. Monet, Degas and their contemporaries launched a heated debate about the role of painting in the modern world that would pave the way for just about every modern art movement of note

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England v Canada (Kick-off 7.15pm). All the action from the friendly at bet365 Stadium in Stoke. The sides last met in April 2019, when a goal by the world's all-time leading international goalscorer Christine Sinclair gave Canada a 1-0 win at the Academy Stadium in Manchester, and the Lionesses will be expecting another tough test this evening

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The crime documentary reveals how a 1970s miscarriage of justice, involving three teenage boys, led to a new method of police interrogation and investigation. When the body of Maxwell Confait was found in south London, three local boys confessed to arson and murder and were consequently convicted, despite retracting their statements and protesting their innocence. However, each of their convictions were eventually overturned, which shone a light on dangerous police practices that pressured the boys to confess to a crime they did not commit

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: When a lonely estate agent sells a house to a charismatic social media influencer, the two strike up an unlikely friendship. But before long her obsession with the woman's seemingly perfect world intensifies, and the lines between online and reality become dangerously blurred. Dark comedy, starring Leanne Best

programme.title: Colour: The Spectrum of Science
programme.desc: Helen Czerski examines the complexities of colour, including what it is, how it works, and how it has written the story of the planet. In the first edition, she looks for the colours that have made the earth multi-coloured, investigating the essence of sunlight, how vivid blue is formed from sulphur atoms, and why the presence of red ochre is a key sign of life. She also looks at why gold should not even exist on the surface of the planet, and the role of salt and water in shaping planet earth

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: The critic tells the story of Australian movie-making, focusing on the films that capture this idiosyncratic nation with drama, emotion and humour. This episode sees how Aussie film-makers gained the confidence to tell their own stories with the boldness of Picnic at Hanging Rock and Mad Max, the global success of Crocodile Dundee and Shine, the flamboyance of Strictly Ballroom and the raw authenticity of Samson and Delilah

programme.title: Nolan: Australia's Maverick Artist
programme.desc: Profile of painter Sidney Nolan, exploring his humble origins before becoming an innovative member of the modernist movement, creating passionate depictions of his home country's iconography. The programme explores his travels round the world and how he eventually became part of a London-based community of artists, and considers how his experimental work may have inspired future generations

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The crime documentary reveals how a 1970s miscarriage of justice, involving three teenage boys, led to a new method of police interrogation and investigation. When the body of Maxwell Confait was found in south London, three local boys confessed to arson and murder and were consequently convicted, despite retracting their statements and protesting their innocence. However, each of their convictions were eventually overturned, which shone a light on dangerous police practices that pressured the boys to confess to a crime they did not commit

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best continues to explore lost railway routes in Northern Ireland, visiting lines that ran through towns and villages around Lough Neagh

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures a mountain at the height of winter, beneath one of his trademark cool blue skies

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: Five-part documentary about the scandal that toppled a president, beginning with the 1972 break-in at the Democratic Party's headquarters at the Watergate Hotel

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: The documentary continues with Richard Nixon's efforts to cover his tracks following the break-in at the Democrat headquarters

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: A young Congolese woman begins to tell her story online - words of survival and solidarity - despite growing up in a culture that insists these words must never be spoken. Drama, written and performed by Dorcas Sebuyange

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin examines the toothbrush, an item so ubiquitous that most people do not give it a moment's thought - and yet 200 million of them are thrown away each year. Zoe sets out to create a sustainable solution, heading to a low-impact factory in Sweden where handles are made from sugarcane bio-based plastic and exploring bespoke production techniques employing 3D laser sintering

programme.title: Peter Rice: An Engineer Imagines
programme.desc: A tribute to the life and ideas of Peter Rice, one of the most distinguished structural engineers of the late 20th century. The film traces his career from his native Ireland through London, Sydney and Paris, revealing how he pushed the boundaries of art and science, until his untimely death in 1992

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The oil palm is a remarkable tree, with an incredibly diverse fruit that can be used for many different things - from shampoo and soap to toothpaste, crisps and chocolate. Unfortunately, the spread of this remarkable plant is leading to the destruction of the rainforests, particularly in Borneo and Sumatra. So what can we do? Firstly, learn where your food is coming from. Pay attention to the labels and look out for sustainable palm oil. We can’t get rid of palm oil altogether as it is in countless products, and many of the alternatives would be worse. But there are different ways of making palm oil, some of which will not completely destroy the forests. The next thing you could do is buy some forest for yourself. It is possible to fundraise, to get money to purchase a piece of perfect rainforest and protect it. So why not see if you can wash cars, and buy a piece of forest the size of your classroom, the size of your assembly hall, possibly even the size of your school. Finally, think

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Arlo, Oliver, Elijah, Zuri and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag are colourful sheets of sandpaper, and all the parents and little ones have different objects and toys that feel rough. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding a safe, baby-appropriate rough object or toy inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The nursery rhyme Michael Finnegan features, as does a story involving Baby Bear and Baby Tiger, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring rough things too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Freya the Frog has just taken her first leap out of the tropical pond. She’s leaving all the tadpoles behind to explore the big wide world. She’s ready for anything, but her confidence gets her into a sticky situation. After all, she is a very tiny frog in a very big garden. Luckily, Freya meets Sid the Snail and Wilbur the Worm on the way, and they help her on her journey.

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour yellow, from cheese to chicks, and we explore yellow in spring time. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour yellow.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 15-month-old Francis and Esme as they discover the world around them and copy what they see. At home, Francis has a go at turning pages while he reads a book with mum, and Esme goes to a baby yoga class and copies mum and the other babies, stretching and bouncing to the music. At the same time, Francis watches his sister getting her shoes and coat on, and has a go before heading out to his baby class! Finally, Esme has a go at Simon Says and Francis practices his animal noises while playing with his puzzle board. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Holly comes from the English city, Liverpool. She will be making two tasty recipes. The first is a local Merseyside stew called scouse, made with chopped and cooked carrots, potatoes and onions with gravy, cooked beef, bay leaves and her special ingredient: celery. The second is a side of crusty bread. Holly makes a dough and stirs in yoghurt and honey. Then it is topped with more yoghurt and pumpkin seeds and placed in the oven. Holly also serves pickled beetroot and cabbage, a traditional side to scouse. Will Holly's friends enjoy her delicious food, and will they enjoy the celery once it is cooked in her recipe?

programme.desc: Annie and Alex are two young swans trying to work out what they want to be when they grow up. Alex wants to be a good singer, just like Jago the Robin with his beautiful song. Annie wants to be on the catwalk like Simon the Stickleback, who has a vibrant sense of colour. But they’re not sure they’ll ever be able to do either of those things. Will they ever discover what they want to be?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 16-month-old Isaac and Ije as they discover the world around them and make magical memories. Ije visits an observatory with her family and draws all the amazing things she can see, then goes to a photo shoot with her family, ready to strike a pose! Isaac and his family are also taking photos to remember their day. Isaac plays with a shape toy, learning and remembering the different shapes. Ije’s family are now in the park, where they have a picnic and play snap. Isaac is in the garden with dad, where he plays finders keepers with painted rocks. Ije and mum put all of her mementos in a scrapbook, looking back at their day out. Finally, Isaac sits with mum and dad, sticking their photos from their day into a book to remember. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Leo's family is from Russia, and they have shown him how to make a special sharlotka, a simple Russian apple cake. Leo starts by separating the eggs. He then adds sugar and flour to the yolks and mixes them together until the mixture is smooth. He whisks the egg whites until they are stiff and stirs them into the yolk mix. After this, Leo cuts apples into small chunks and places half of them in a cake tin, pours over the egg mixture and puts the rest of the apples on top. Then the sharlotka is ready to be baked in the oven. Leo even has an extra special Russian-themed surprise for his friends while they eat! Can they work out what his special ingredient is?

programme.desc: In this episode, we follow Amardas as he celebrates the Sikh festival of Vaisakhi with his family. Amardas attends the Punjabi school where he learns all about the festival and why it’s important to Sikhs. After learning all about Vaisakhi, Armardas heads home to help his mum cook delicious treats for the big ceremony at the Gurdwara. Once he arrives at the Gurdwara, he begins by helping to lower and clean the flag; the Nishan Sahib. When the flag is clean, Armadas joins his dad on stage to drum and worship. Here we join Armardas as he tells us about the five ‘K’s and why they’re important to Sikhs.

programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘tractor’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see all sorts of tractors – a blue tractor, a pulling tractor, a stuck-in-the-mud tractor and a farm tractor. There’s a big tractor, a chug-along tractor, a party tractor and even a trumpeting tractor. Dee and a friend also spy a whopping wheels tractor. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Inside the potting shed, Betty the Mouse is trying to get her three youngsters to sleep, but a strange noise is keeping them all awake. Betty sets off to find out where the noise is coming from. She bumps into Fred the Pill Millipede, who is practicing his tap dancing. Is that the noise? Or maybe it’s Sid the Snail practicing his meditation? Betty needs to find out if she’s ever to get her little ones to sleep.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
