programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour orange, from oranges to orangutans, and explore the deeper meaning of the colour. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour orange.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 17-month-old Alexander and Cianna as they discover the world around them and explore one step at a time. Alexander starts at home, playing a game using balls and two hula hoops - what does he get up to? Cianna plays in the garden with her family, having a go at stacking colourful rings - but does she know what order they go in? Alexander heads out to play his very first game of mini golf. His club is as tall as he is! Meanwhile, Cianna and her brother have some splashing fun at a local park before collecting some leaves to do leaf printing at home. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Suri's family are from Iran, a large country in the Middle East in the continent of Asia. She makes the traditional Iranian dish of chicken joojeh. Suri begins by marinating the chicken in natural yoghurt, spring onions, turmeric powder and lemon juice. She then takes her special ingredient of saffron, grinds it down to a powder, and adds salt and natural yoghurt before she mixes it in with the rest of the marinade. Suri serves her chicken joojeh with tomatoes. They enjoy the meal while sitting on the floor, a traditional Iranian custom on special occasions. Will her friends like the special ingredient when it is cooked in her joojeh?

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Harsimrat and Jagjit celebrate the arrival of their first-born baby at home, while 15-year-old Ansh learns about the sacred meaning of his turban. Newlyweds Hari and Kiran continue their devotion to their guru through their marriage and in their daily lives, and Hardeep scatters his father's ashes over flowing water to unite him with God

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman lets Cody in on a family secret. Dylan gets caught up in Zane's conflict and Norma joins forces with a stranger in the anti-bypass campaign

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman and Norma's relationship is put in jeopardy by a growing secret and Emma finds herself in a dangerous situation. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why astronaut Tim Peake's space capsule has landed in the city's 900-year-old cathedral

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Labour Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers embark on another search for outstanding homes across Scotland, beginning in the Scottish Borders. Properties include a blue house in Biggar, a wavy new build near Peebles and a home to motorbike enthusiasts in Castle Douglas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Kush plans a huge surprise for Whitney, his mood is brought down when Gray reminds him that by accepting Phil's extra charges he will be going to prison for a very long time. Keegan proudly sets up his new market stall - but what is Tiffany hiding? Karen receives an unusual proposition but takes it the wrong way

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil decides to enhance the hotel's reputation with an exclusive night of gourmet cuisine. His new chef has a cordon bleu rating and loves every type of dish - but he is particularly taken with Manuel. Classic comedy, written by and starring John Cleese and Connie Booth. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the climate activist over the course of 2019 and 2020, as she travels the world exploring the science of global warming and challenging world leaders to take action on the growing crisis. The programme follows the impact of Covid-19 on her campaign, and the challenges of persuading a world living through one crisis to face up to the possibility of another on the horizon. In the first programme, Greta travels through North America on her way to a UN climate conference in Chile

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler with the latest news and analysis from the election campaign

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: The couple clash at home before a big family trip to New York. Back in Bolton it's 'new hair who dis' for Faryal before an emotional chat about Amir's plans for one last dance

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Amir puts a pause on training for a quick trip to Dubai for some holiday home shopping. Back in Bolton, the wedding hall is causing more headache and Amir fears the worst.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home from South Africa, the latest potential buyer has a budget of £450,000 and heads west to Cornwall, with Steve Brown leading the search for a character property that has holiday let potential. Steve also pays a visit to the Newlyn Copper Works and has a bash at transforming the striking reddish metal into a delicate work of art

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by two best friends, one of whom likes traditional cooking while the other is more adventurous

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is desperate to find Kush, terrified he has run away before his court appearance. The Carters are determined to make Frankie's birthday special by throwing a party at the Vic, where an unexpected guest surprises everyone. A mistake on social media puts the spotlight on Keegan

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The outcome of his compensation case against the hospital inspires Dominic to refashion his life, but when his plans are dashed, he's soon asking a huge favour of Sacha. Kian's bromance with a troubled patient takes a surprising turn when he and Chloe make a shocking discovery in theatre, while money matters continue to mount for Max

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A 95-year-old navy veteran is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He is diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. He is also 100 miles from hospital, where the nightshift is about to get very busy. Meanwhile, the dental team takes to the road to reach patients across Shetland

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: After hours of searching Monaco, the kennel colleagues track Frank to his hotel. Now is their chance to force him to admit he is living the high life on their winnings - only for their plan to be scuppered when the security guards throw them out of the hotel. Back in Yorkshire, the remaining staff are struggling to keep the understaffed kennels running smoothly, while Colette worries that Richard and his wealthy business associates will never accept her

programme.title: Finding Faith with Stephen Nolan
programme.desc: The presenter talks to people from different cultures who believe in God about their faith. Stephen has never believed in God, and considers it both an unreasoning position and a useful source of stability in the lives of those who do. Beginning in his home town of Shankill, he reflects on how religion never became a present aspect in his life and talks to people about the events that led them to find God

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Sophie Duker joins Neil Delamere, Colin Murphy and Joanne McNally as they raise a socially distanced laugh or two in response to current events. Collectively, they wag the finger of blame at everyone and everything which has caught their eye from the people and events that have been making the headlines. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home from South Africa, the latest potential buyer has a budget of £450,000 and heads west to Cornwall, with Steve Brown leading the search for a character property that has holiday let potential. Steve also pays a visit to the Newlyn Copper Works and has a bash at transforming the striking reddish metal into a delicate work of art

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by two best friends, one of whom likes traditional cooking while the other is more adventurous

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is desperate to find Kush, terrified he has run away before his court appearance. The Carters are determined to make Frankie's birthday special by throwing a party at the Vic, where an unexpected guest surprises everyone. A mistake on social media puts the spotlight on Keegan

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The outcome of his compensation case against the hospital inspires Dominic to refashion his life, but when his plans are dashed, he's soon asking a huge favour of Sacha. Kian's bromance with a troubled patient takes a surprising turn when he and Chloe make a shocking discovery in theatre, while money matters continue to mount for Max

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A 95-year-old navy veteran is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He is diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. He is also 100 miles from hospital, where the nightshift is about to get very busy. Meanwhile, the dental team takes to the road to reach patients across Shetland

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: After hours of searching Monaco, the kennel colleagues track Frank to his hotel. Now is their chance to force him to admit he is living the high life on their winnings - only for their plan to be scuppered when the security guards throw them out of the hotel. Back in Yorkshire, the remaining staff are struggling to keep the understaffed kennels running smoothly, while Colette worries that Richard and his wealthy business associates will never accept her

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Harsimrat and Jagjit celebrate the arrival of their first-born baby at home, while 15-year-old Ansh learns about the sacred meaning of his turban. Newlyweds Hari and Kiran continue their devotion to their guru through their marriage and in their daily lives, and Hardeep scatters his father's ashes over flowing water to unite him with God

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman lets Cody in on a family secret. Dylan gets caught up in Zane's conflict and Norma joins forces with a stranger in the anti-bypass campaign

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman and Norma's relationship is put in jeopardy by a growing secret and Emma finds herself in a dangerous situation. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why astronaut Tim Peake's space capsule has landed in the city's 900-year-old cathedral

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Home Cook Heroes
programme.desc: Suzie makes a selection of pork dishes, using the best quality Northern Irish meat sourced from County Down. She creates a Cantonese feast of crispy pork belly and sweet and succulent Char Sui pork, demonstrates how easy it is to make sausage rolls and finishes with a tangy upside down rhubarb and ginger cake

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: Dennis recalls his youth in Coalisland and rise on the professional snooker circuit. While Dennis often reached tournament finals, he always seemed to falter - leading the press to class him as an eternal runner-up. In 1985, however, everything changed with Dennis going on to make the ultimate sporting comeback in a World Championship final watched by a record-breaking 18.5 million people. He talks to Stephen Watson about his upbringing, the World Championship title win - and the origin of those famous upside-down glasses

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: A to Z of Parliament
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a guide on what it takes to make a MP's Maiden Speech in the House of Commons

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: No Say Over My Body
programme.desc: Dozens of migrant women have alleged that they faced unnecessarily invasive gynaecological procedures without proper consent whilst being held at a US immigration detention centre. programme.desc: The friends organise a picnic, but forget to pack a dessert, leaving Patches to save the day

programme.desc: Gaelic songs and stories, featuring the letter B

programme.desc: The duo investigate light and dark

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup

programme.desc: Matilda has the power to freely re-interpret the classic tales. She gets together with Curlybeard, Mortimer and Charlie to tell them a ‘special’ version of ‘The Wizard of Oz'

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The eagle has long been regarded as the king of birds. Historically mankind has used the eagle as a symbol of strength and courage. Cailean Maclean explores the myths and history that surrounds these majestic birds of prey

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Robert Robertson presents music from the Trad Awards archives, featuring Phil Cunningham, Aly Bain & Violet Tulloch, and Innes Watson and band

programme.title: Guth Ag Aithris - Christine Primrose

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Roddy cooks up Caribbean chicken with rice and peas and a Hungarian goulash with dumplings, while Uisdean rattles off a five-minute steak dish and a tasty pan-fried hake with mussels

programme.desc: A big storm causes a lot of damage on the island of Struay, and Uncle Matthew's home is one of the buildings damaged

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach (Fox & Hare)
programme.desc: Fox finds a giant dice in a box of discarded belongings. Owl really likes it too and decides to trade it for his favourite doll, Oehoe

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane and Sam Spratt embark on a woodland mission, as they try to prove that Eddy is capable of finding them some truffles

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Rainbowfish explains to us how they are schooling fish, how they enjoy being together and that they become more colourful when they are with their friends

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The friends enjoy a day at the beach, while Ela searches for a stile

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Iain Macaulay reads A’ Chaora a Ghur Isean, written and illustrated by Gemma Merino and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia and Biorc are unhappy with the situation at the Fort and they come to the decision that they would be happier if they both moved out into the forest together

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: A chance to meet Coinneach Clogaid and Olaidh Orains, and we come upon an exclamation mark in a strange place

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Fetije lives in Belgium but her family are Albanian, leaving her confused about identity

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: It’s staff appreciation day at Danger HQ, and Squawk and Danger Mouse have two very different ideas on where to go

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Mairi MacRitchie samples an Italian stromboli bread, with a crisp crust and tasty filling of artichokes, olives and cheese. In her own kitchen, she changes a traditional tarte tatin to a delicious savoury dish using red onions and adds thyme to a moist lemon bundt cake for a special twist

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about uses of water, including ice baths, cliff jumping and divination

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Wild Atlantic Way
programme.desc: Anne Lundon takes to the road for a journey along Ireland's spectacular Wild Atlantic Way

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: the climax of the International competition, which takes place on a large field with a long outrun, and poses a challenge for the most experienced of dogs

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The eagle has long been regarded as the king of birds. Historically mankind has used the eagle as a symbol of strength and courage. Cailean Maclean explores the myths and history that surrounds these majestic birds of prey

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: A short-sighted traffic warden causes chaos in the town, and it is up to the residents of Fraochy Bay to come up with a creative solution

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon reads a parable and introduces hymns and psalms, and the singers featured include Catherine Tinney, Seona Brown and Iain 'Spanish' MacKay

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile visit a castle and find out that a castle is a big, important house where royal people live

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has a shiny orange balloon, but it is not long until this beloved new toy pops

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Charlie visits today, but he and Bing cannot agree on what to play

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The dolphin reveals how clever they are and how only humans can claim to be smarter

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: Rickety teaches the Rangers a new game in order to teach them tracking skills

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: The friends organise a picnic, but forget to pack a dessert, leaving Patches to save the day

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Marie MacAulay reads Tha an Tidsear Agam Coltach ri UILEBHÈIST, written and illustrated by Peter Brown and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: The palace is attacked while the Furious Five are away, and Po is forced to seek help from an unexpected ally

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Olaidh Orains and Màiri Bhàn nan Casan Fada have lots of dance moves as they ice skate, but her long hair leaves them in knots

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make films on a budget

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Cases include keyhole surgery on a coati, a vet chiropractor treats dogs and a rabbit, and surgery on a dog

programme.title: Speaking Our Language

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of customs officers

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Am Posadh Afraganach/The African Wedding
programme.desc: Humanitarian Gemma Steele and her husband Sean MacKinnon make a special trip for a second wedding celebration with her other family in Kenya

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay immerses himself in Durness’s history, mainland Britain’s most north-westerly community, surrounded by brilliant beaches and stunning sea lochs

programme.title: Sgeulachd Edward Pritchard - The Human Crocodile
programme.desc: Documentary examining the story of Dr Edward Pritchard, who became the last person to be hanged in a public execution when he was convicted for two murders by poisoning in 1865. Presented by John Morrison, from the scenes of the doctor's crimes in Glasgow city centre

programme.title: Na Trads

programme.title: Good Morning Britain

programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Sally Lindsay hosts as a team of family and friends attempts about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith makes the best of an opportunity. Gabby is concerned for Kim. Charles is confused by forthcoming arrangements

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick fills Sarah and David in on why Leanne has to move away and so forces him to choose between her and Sam. Peter is stunned when the consultant reveals that he may not be eligible for a liver transplant. Steve asks Dev to stop stealing the Kabin's business

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Strictly Come Dancing professional dancer Anton Du Beke, who discusses cheering up the nation this summer, and horticulturist Camilla Bassett-Smith opens up her cottage garden. Plus, there is a look at birds of prey

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick fills Sarah and David in on why Leanne has to move away and so forces him to choose between her and Sam. Peter is stunned when the consultant reveals that he may not be eligible for a liver transplant. Steve asks Dev to stop stealing the Kabin's business

programme.title: Too Close
programme.desc: Forensic psychiatrist Emma Robertson is assigned to work with Connie Mortensen, a wife and mother accused of a despicable crime. Her assessment of the woman's sanity will determine whether she faces life in jail or a secure psychiatric hospital, or the chance of rehabilitation and release. Psychological thriller, starring Emily Watson and Denise Gough

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: Euston is evacuated during Friday rush hour - leaving passengers there and in Birmingham New Street station stranded, confused and angry. Driver Andy reveals just how close his train came to disaster on the tracks and train dispatcher and former bank manager David imparts his worldly wisdom to a new recruit on his first shift. A train runs a red signal at Birmingham, so engineers have to work through the night to move the stranded locomotive, investigate the cause and get things back up and running before the morning rush

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Tens of thousands of Newcastle United fans pass through the Metro for a home game on the first day of the new football season - and many of them are far from pleased with the club's owner. A guide horse undergoes training on the Metro system before moving to London, and in Sunderland, a crackdown on fare dodgers gets underway

Ed tells Michael that he spent the night at the builder's yard catching up on paperwork

programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets an energetic Staffie cross who needs help calming down in kennels, falls in love with a rare West African dog called an Azawakh, and coaxes an overbred bulldog to come out of its shell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz assures Tyrone she is not looking for a romantic reunion, but to give the girls some stability. Over a glass of wine, Gail assures Natasha that she's family now, no matter what happens between her and Nick

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning

programme.title: Loose Women

programme.title: Judge Rinder

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Alex, Kelley, Tony and Jade pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Party Election Broadcast

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A tearful Hope asks Fiz why Daddy has left them. Abi puts pressure on Kevin to talk to Tyrone about moving out, and Brian reports Cathy's online abuse to Craig, who is apologetic but explains there's nothing the police can do. Ed tells Michael that he spent the night at the builder's yard catching up on paperwork

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets an energetic Staffie cross who needs help calming down in kennels, falls in love with a rare West African dog called an Azawakh, and coaxes an overbred bulldog to come out of its shell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz assures Tyrone she is not looking for a romantic reunion, but to give the girls some stability. Tyrone tells Alina that he is moving back in with Fiz on a temporary basis, and when Cathy asserts that it would be best if she moved away, Brian begs her not to leave. Over a glass of wine, Gail assures Natasha that she's family now, no matter what happens between her and Nick

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (2009)
programme.desc: The young wizard is given the task of uncovering the secrets of Lord Voldemort's past as the evil sorcerer's henchmen cause havoc in both the magical and Muggle worlds. With the help of headmaster Dumbledore, Harry ventures into the memories of those who knew the Dark Lord during his early years, hoping to gain an insight into the secret behind his apparent immortality. However, Voldemort's influence extends much closer to home than expected, as Harry's rival Malfoy is chosen to carry out a sinister mission. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Michael Gambon, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Tom Felton and Alan Rickman

With the help of headmaster Dumbledore, Harry ventures into the memories of those who knew the Dark Lord during his early years, hoping to gain an insight into the secret behind his apparent immortality. However, Voldemort's influence extends much closer to home than expected, as Harry's rival Malfoy is chosen to carry out a sinister mission. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Michael Gambon, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Tom Felton and Alan Rickman

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack asks Arthur's sister out to the Boxing Day social - much to her brother's displeasure. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack is offered the job of bus inspector and Stan is promised extra overtime, for which he will not have to work - as well as a glamorous conductress. Reg Varney and Bob Grant star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Things may be about to change for Nick when a new inspector arrives and Kate receives a job offer. Elsewhere, Greengrass struggles with his finances. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is determined to go it alone. Kelly asks Lisa for a pay rise for Roy. Zak loses his temper with Kelly and Paddy. The Windsors' party gets underway

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Scott is determined to get himself a job. Paddy spends his first night at the Dingles. Chris is annoyed that Reynolds has undercut him again. Zoe has some bad news for Paddy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly panics about his speech. Leanne is jealous of Nick's relationship with Gail. Fiona is annoyed when Pam tells Steve to stay away from her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Ann Smith, a 53-year-old grandmother from Watford, is haunted by unanswered questions about her past. As a baby, Ann was adopted by George and Jean Smith, but despite growing up in a loving family, Ann was always aware that she was different, and began the search for her birth mother. Meanwhile, 73-year-old Derek Berg searches for his brother who was given up by his parents. His mother Freda had an affair with an Allied soldier, and like many other illegitimate wartime babies, Derek's long lost sibling was put up for adoption

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth

programme.desc: Roy does not know how to react to Hayley's news. Alma is put out when Mike tells Deirdre she can stay with them. Chris and Samantha make plans to see each other again

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve encourages Fiona to plan a night out. Deirdre moves in with the Baldwins. Judy urges Gary to end his partnership with Jim and get some work from Steve

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of prison officer Charlie Tyner, Brooks and Devlin discover he had been abusing his position and convicts in his charge. A suspect is brought to trial, but Steel and Phillips go in pursuit of a career criminal connected to Tyner and have to persuade their key witness to give evidence against a man she claims to love. Starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff takes his mood out on Roy and Marlon. Paddy's mum pleads with him to visit but he refuses. Rachel confronts Graham about the photo

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy is inconsolable about his mum. Mandy is convinced that Mrs Kirk is lying. Rachel wonders if she is a bad mother

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly grows weary of Spider's protests. Deirdre is in a dark place and hates herself for trusting Jon. Hayley tells Alma that she used to be a man

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally gets annoyed when Kevin objects to Chris babysitting. Spider and Emily resume their protest outside Firman's. Deirdre is stunned when Jon tracks her down

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team embark on a race against time to recapture a dangerous convict who has escaped from Bodmin prison - a case that leads them to an elaborate drugs racket. Starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Sean Chapman

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The young detective is suspended from duty following a recent case. However, he is roused from his bitterness and self-pity when the body of a woman who went missing at a funfair on Cowley Green is found near his home. He resolves to conduct a secret investigation of his own, which leads him to the city's social elite and a disturbing secret hidden in plain sight. Shaun Evans stars as the young Inspector Morse in the third series of the detective drama

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha decides to leave Weatherfield and move to London. Fiona struggles to keep control of her emotions. Roy tries to build up the courage to declare his love for Hayley

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona brings Morgan home to her flat. Gail is furious when Leanne gatecrashes her family meal. Curly asks Spider to help him write his speech about the environment

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa and Mandy try to convince Butch to agree to an annulment. Stella reveals how she got her fortune. Chris and Terry discover Reynolds is in serious financial trouble

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly plans to spend Roy's wages on furniture. Eric confronts Stella about her winnings. Zak and Butch argue about Mandy. Scott tries to put things right with Roy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Tongues start wagging when Greengrass goes into business with a stranger from America, while Kate has her work cut out looking after a flu-stricken Nick. Rural drama, starring Bill Maynard and Nick Berry

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack asks Arthur's sister out to the Boxing Day social - much to her brother's displeasure. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack is offered the job of bus inspector and Stan is promised extra overtime, for which he will not have to work - as well as a glamorous conductress. Reg Varney and Bob Grant star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Things may be about to change for Nick when a new inspector arrives and Kate receives a job offer. Elsewhere, Greengrass struggles with his finances. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is determined to go it alone. Kelly asks Lisa for a pay rise for Roy. Zak loses his temper with Kelly and Paddy. The Windsors' party gets underway

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Scott is determined to get himself a job. Paddy spends his first night at the Dingles. Chris is annoyed that Reynolds has undercut him again. Zoe has some bad news for Paddy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly panics about his speech. Leanne is jealous of Nick's relationship with Gail. Fiona is annoyed when Pam tells Steve to stay away from her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy invites Hayley for a meal at the cafe. She tells Roy she really likes him, but also has a confession to make. Samantha is desperate for some space from Des

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Ann Smith, a 53-year-old grandmother from Watford, is haunted by unanswered questions about her past. As a baby, Ann was adopted by George and Jean Smith, but despite growing up in a loving family, Ann was always aware that she was different, and began the search for her birth mother. Meanwhile, 73-year-old Derek Berg searches for his brother who was given up by his parents. His mother Freda had an affair with an Allied soldier, and like many other illegitimate wartime babies, Derek's long lost sibling was put up for adoption

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.desc: Figgis is dismissive when Archie and Norman play a game of poker, believing that skill has nothing to do with it and it is all really down to luck. Comedy, with James Bolam and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Robin is bewitched by evil sorceress Lilith, who sends him on a mission to steal the magical silver arrow from Herne's cave. Michael Praed stars

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar has to undergo training to become a mercenary in an attempt to foil a hijacking. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo investigate when Harriet's father is the victim of a cunning burglar who sneaks in undetected and steals a priceless jade collection. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley and the team are confused by a chain of events that involves lethal narcotics, a series of killings and a drug addict. As they piece the clues together, Bodie and Doyle realise someone has a plan to flood the public water supply with lethal hallucenogenic drugs. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A professional gambler needs to hide a group of people who might be able to help him win at roulette, so Arthur hits on the idea of using Terry's flat. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring a pre-Birds of a Feather Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner go on a Croatian adventure, searching for huge king carp, culminating in them visiting the home of the Croatian carp record of 94lb

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. Robin and the outlaws leave Sherwood to defend a distant village from a band of phantom horsemen known as the Hounds of Lucifer, who terrorise the countryside on the orders of a powerful coven ruled by Morgwyn of Ravenscar. Guest starring Rula Lenska

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar helps a businessman who claims he is being ruined by an imposter. Classic adventures, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Jim reveals his gentler side when Harriet's friend is found murdered, but the case is clouded when the victim's vengeful father gets in the way. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the murder of a prostitute, but their only clues are a pornographic film and a Eastern bloc-backed plot to blackmail a prominent politician tipped to become a future prime minister. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry minds a boxer, but is less than impressed to discover the promoter is betting on his opponent to win the next bout. Arthur, meanwhile, switches bets. Alfred Marks, Paul Barber and Vicki Michelle guest star

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Fisherman Ali Hamidi is joined by Tom Dove and Neil Spooner to catch buffalo carp in Texas

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find a nine-year-old boy on his doorstep claiming to be his son, and ends up with a minding job he had not anticipated. He forms a bond with the youngster, eventually taking his side against his stepfather. Sharon Duce guest stars, with Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner go on a Croatian adventure, searching for huge king carp, culminating in them visiting the home of the Croatian carp record of 94lb

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Susie Dent, Anthony Crolla, Ellie Taylor and Harry Redknapp pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Meanwhile, Customer Service Agent Mr Sho is having issues with a 400-year-old Japanese tradition

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Virgin Atlantic's punctuality record is put in jeopardy by a captain's bottle of water, and there is love - actually in the air

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Riggs and Murtaugh respond to the murder of an escaped patient of Dr Cahill's, and the question of the medic's competency is brought into question. Family tensions run high between Murtaugh and Trish, who need to make some big decisions. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.desc: An expert sniper with a high-powered imported rifle holds a family hostage in a high-rise flat, planning to target a royal visitor at the Wimbledon tennis tournament. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to a remote river in the Australian Outback in a bid to catch the rare and fearsome freshwater sawfish, which is reputed to reach 18ft long and attack boats. In his quest to discover more about the predator, he roams across an ancient landscape, encountering scientists and a real-life Crocodile Dundee, before finally confronting the disturbing truth

programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find a nine-year-old boy on his doorstep claiming to be his son, and ends up with a minding job he had not anticipated. He forms a bond with the youngster, eventually taking his side against his stepfather. Sharon Duce guest stars, with Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner go on a Croatian adventure, searching for huge king carp, culminating in them visiting the home of the Croatian carp record of 94lb

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Robin is trapped on a lonely hillside by the Sheriff's men, and sacrifices his life so that Much and Marian can escape. Michael Praed stars, making his last appearance as the legendary hero

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin present coverage of the 3.35 Nell Gwynn Stakes from Newmarket, which follows races at 2.25 and 3.00, plus two races from Cheltenham. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Matt Chapman, and commentary from Rishi Persad

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV, including horse racing, Formula One and darts

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An expert sniper with a high-powered imported rifle holds a family hostage in a high-rise flat, planning to target a royal visitor at the Wimbledon tennis tournament. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to a remote river in the Australian Outback in a bid to catch the rare and fearsome freshwater sawfish, which is reputed to reach 18ft long and attack boats. In his quest to discover more about the predator, he roams across an ancient landscape, encountering scientists and a real-life Crocodile Dundee, before finally confronting the disturbing truth

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner travel from the hustle and bustle of Marrakesh to the Atlas mountains which is the setting for a mythical visit to a huge reservoir

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Angellica Bell, Oritse Williams, Leslie Ash and Ian Lavender pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: A routine trip to the dentist ends disastrously for Bean, and a quiet stroll in the park gradually turns into a pitched battle with a wasp. Rowan Atkinson stars, with appearances by Richard Wilson and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's clumsy alter ego returns to cause more chaos as he puts his dubious first aid skills to good use on an unconscious man in the street, makes a meal out of the simple task of posting a letter, and jets off for the holiday of a lifetime

programme.title: Rocky V (1990)
programme.desc: The heavyweight champion is forced into retirement due to brain damage inflicted in the ring, and finds his fortune has been lost on the stock market by his accountant. Returning to his working-class roots, he trains an up-and-coming fighter, only to see his protege turn against him. Drama sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Talia Shire and real-life boxer Tommy Morrison

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Morland Sanders reports on the future of workers' rights in the UK in the wake of lockdown and departure from the EU. The programme examines evidence of safety breaches, low pay, and dangerous pressures at the heart of the gig economy, and revealing how gig-based working practices are becoming more commonplace, even appearing in organisations like the NHS

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
