The Brimicombes and their children will see their weekly budget increase from £140 to a massive £3,000, and will also enjoy nightly takeaways, three cars, regular pampering sessions and expensive shopping trips. Meanwhile, Jo is away on business so Steve will be left as a single parent - how will he cope given he has never even worked a washing machine?

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Two more families swap homes, budgets and lifestyles for a week to see how the other half lives. For Matthew and Belinda Sethard-Wright and their two children, this means exchanging their large house in Orpington for a Bromley council house. Moving into their home will be Skye Walker and her three children. As a professional housekeeper, Skye works in houses not unlike the Sethard-Wrights'. Now she will be able to enjoy its many comforts for herself - include a swimming pool, tennis court and weekly £1,500 shopping budget

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers Gav Hall and Dan Mottishaw are in hot pursuit of a suspected drug dealer, but the two-wheeled tearaway runs out of road, flips his bike over and crashes into a hedge. Elsewhere, a man wanted for ABH gives interceptors Lewis Marshall and Darren Chalk the runaround, and Jen Ellse pulls over a suspected uninsured driver, who struggles to explain the fake plates in his boot

programme.title: Here Come the Gypsies!
programme.desc: Revealing the hidden world of Gypsy and Traveller communities, delving into their unique traditions and codes to understand how this community continues to thrive. In South Wales, Romany Gypsy horse dealer Jim 'Beb' Price faces his toughest day of the year as he puts on his first ever horse drive. Meanwhile in Surrey, Romany Gypsy cage fighter Tony 'The Rhino' Giles arranges a bare-knuckle fight to settle a dispute between two warring Gypsy families

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War with the Law
programme.desc: The security personnel at Coventry's West Orchards shopping centre receive a tip-off from Birmingham that an organised criminal gang is heading their way. In Weston Favell shopping centre in Northamptonshire, security guards are under pressure when a special event brings in extra shoppers, but an elusive serial shoplifter is apprehended stealing a trolley full of goods before disappearing amongst the crowds

programme.title: Police: Suspect No 1
programme.desc: After a raid on a cannabis factory, nine people are arrested and it is up to the team at Norwich CID to get to the bottom of which role each suspect played. In the interview room, different stories emerge and the detectives face a daunting challenge to determine who is telling the truth and who is spinning lies

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler catches a glimpse of Rachel in the nude - so she promptly demands he bare all to redress the balance. Meanwhile, Joey is disturbed to hear his father is having an affair. With a guest appearance by Brenda Vaccaro

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer heads off round the Caribbean in mega cruise ship MSC Divina. She and her fellow passengers stop off in Nassau in the Bahamas, where Jane finds solace in the state's oldest Baptist church, and St Martin, where she seeks an intimate underwater audience with a host of sharks. Back on board, Jane is startled by the scale of everything - from the food and activities to the sheer number of people

programme.title: Chasing Cotton Clouds
programme.desc: A young boy creates a fantasy world to help cope with the alienation he feels at home and at school

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go! (1966)
programme.desc: International Rescue is called in to save the first manned space mission to Mars from sinister hijacker The Hood, and ensure a second attempt goes to plan. Puppet adventure based on Gerry Anderson's TV series, featuring the voices of Shane Rimmer, Ray Barrett, Alexander Davion, Peter Dyneley, Sylvia Anderson and Cliff Richard, voicing a puppet version of himself

programme.title: Teen Wolf (1985)
programme.desc: A teenage misfit is initially dismayed to learn he is a werewolf as a result of a hereditary curse. He finds there are advantages as he becomes a local celebrity, while his enhanced agility makes him the star player of his school's basketball team - but fame quickly goes to his head. Comedy, starring Michael J Fox, James Hampton and Susan Ursitti

programme.title: Harry and the Hendersons (1987)
programme.desc: A hapless family's camping holiday in the mountains is brought to a sudden end when they accidentally hit the legendary Bigfoot with their car. They decide to adopt the endearing creature, but have more trouble than expected trying to keep him concealed and to avoid arousing their neighbours' suspicions. Comedy, starring John Lithgow, Melinda Dillon, David Suchet, Don Ameche and Kevin Peter Hall

programme.title: Three Amigos! (1986)
programme.desc: Three silent Western movie stars are summoned to Mexico for what they think will be a public appearance - only to discover the locals really believe in their heroic abilities and expect them to rid their town of a group of evil bandits. Comedy, starring Steve Martin, Chevy Chase and Martin Short

programme.title: Pacific Rim: Uprising (2018)
programme.desc: Ten years after the Pan Pacific Defense Corps unleashed an army of Jaegers - 25-storey tall robots operated by mind-melded humans - former pilot Jake Pentecost is contacted by estranged sister Mako Mori. She implores him to return to the PPDC to head off the threat posed by Liwen Shao, chief executive of Shao Industries, who intends to replace the Jaegers with unmanned drones. Haunted by ghosts of the past, Jake eventually agrees to mentor a 15-year-old cadet called Amara, whose family were crushed to death during one monster attack. Director Steven S DeKnight's literally blockbusting sci-fi sequel, starring John Boyega, Scott Eastwood and Cailee Spaeny

programme.title: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World (2003)
programme.desc: The captain of a British warship engages in a tense battle of wits and an escalating test of nerves with a French counterpart during the Napoleonic conflict, a pursuit that takes his crew on a dangerous journey along the coast of South America. Seafaring drama, adapted from Patrick O'Brian's novels, starring Russell Crowe, Paul Bettany, James D'Arcy, Billy Boyd and Edward Woodall

programme.title: The Other Woman (2014)
programme.desc: A woman is devastated to learn her boyfriend is actually married, but when she meets his wife, they quickly become friends. When they discover he has a third secret lover, they decide to join forces and teach him a lesson. Comedy, starring Cameron Diaz, Leslie Mann, Kate Upton and Nikolaj Coster-Waldau

programme.title: Submarine (2010)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old boy determined to lose his virginity before he turns 16 becomes infatuated with a feisty classmate. He also has a more pressing mission to undertake as he tries to save his parents' failing marriage, despite his father's apathy and the return of his mother's smooth-talking ex. Comedy drama, with Craig Roberts, Sally Hawkins, Paddy Considine and Noah Taylor

programme.title: Crack in the World (1965)
programme.desc: A dying scientist and his colleagues conduct experiments to harness the Earth's inner energy that seem set to end in disaster. Sci-fi thriller, with Dana Andrews, Janette Scott, Kieron Moore and Alexander Knox

programme.title: Horton Hears a Who! (2008)
programme.desc: A kindly elephant hears a cry of help coming from a speck of dust and discovers the minuscule city of Whoville. As he tries to help its citizens, the other animals begin to question his sanity, and the evil Kangaroo incites them to turn against the do-gooder. Animated adaptation of Dr Seuss's book, with the voices of Jim Carrey, Steve Carell, Carol Burnett, Seth Rogen and Will Arnett

programme.title: Rio 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Parrots Jewel and Blu lead a happy life together in Rio with their three chicks, until they find themselves hurled into an expedition up the Amazon. The journey leads to an encounter with an old enemy, while Blu also has to win the approval of Jewel's fearsome father. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jesse Eisenberg, Anne Hathaway and Jamie Foxx

programme.title: Akeelah and the Bee (2006)
programme.desc: A clever girl from a poor neighbourhood finds school frustrating and tries to hide her intelligence from her classmates. When she competes in a spelling competition, she is surprised to win and with the aid of a strict coach, sets out to take part in a national tournament and provide her community with something to rally round. Drama, starring Keke Palmer, Laurence Fishburne and Angela Bassett

programme.title: Super 8 (2011)
programme.desc: A boy and his friends sneak out at night to film an amateur zombie movie, only to witness a train derailment. As people start disappearing from their town and the military descends in force, they come to realise a dangerous alien was being transported on the train and is now on the loose. Sci-fi thriller, starring Joel Courtney and Elle Fanning

programme.title: The Equalizer 2 (2018)
programme.desc: Robert McCall divides his time between cab driving and looking after a young man who is mixing with a bad crowd. When a CIA friend is murdered, the former black ops agent sets out to avenge her death, and winds up fighting for survival in his hurricane-battered home town. Action thriller, starring Denzel Washington, Pedro Pascal and Ashton Sanders

programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong

programme.title: Ted 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The foul-mouthed talking teddy bear is horrified to discover that he counts as property according to the law. He and his human best friend hire a pot-smoking lawyer to help them fight a court case and get Ted legally recognised as a person. Comedy sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg and Amanda Seyfried, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: The SpongeBob Movie: Sponge Out of Water (2015)
programme.desc: The cheery sea creature tries to prevent a precious secret formula from falling into the wrong hands - but when it goes missing, the end of the world approaches. SpongeBob and his friends embark on a quest to get it back, which leads to them developing superpowers and venturing onto dry land. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny and Antonio Banderas

programme.title: FernGully: The Last Rainforest (1992)
programme.desc: A fairy living in a forest encounters a member of a lumber crew and magically shrinks him down to her size. He learns about the damage humans are doing to the area, and helps defend it when his fellow loggers unwittingly unleash an evil spirit that had been entombed in a tree. Animated adventure, with the voices of Robin Williams, Christian Slater and Tim Curry

programme.title: Over the Hedge (2006)
programme.desc: A raccoon trickster is fretting about his debt to a fearsome bear when he stumbles on some animals waking in the woods after their winter nap. Spotting the chance to solve his problem, the raccoon dupes the gullible creatures into stealing food from a nearby suburb so he can give it to the bear - but they fail to realise they will soon be at the mercy of a vermin exterminator. Animated comedy, with the voices of Bruce Willis, Steve Carell, Garry Shandling and William Shatner

programme.title: The Rewrite (2014)
programme.desc: A washed-up Hollywood screenwriter takes a job at a small university, planning to exploit his position as a local celebrity and do as little work as possible. His cynical outlook begins to change when he falls for a single mother working toward her degree, while a talented pupil makes him consider what he wants from his life. Romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Marisa Tomei, Allison Janney and JK Simmons

programme.title: Exodus: Gods and Kings (2014)
programme.desc: Moses, the adoptive brother of the pharaoh of Egypt, is sent into exile, where he receives a message from God demanding he set all the country's slaves free. When his brother refuses to comply, the kingdom is struck by a series of devastating plagues. Biblical epic, starring Christian Bale, Joel Edgerton, Sigourney Weaver and John Turturro

programme.title: The Greatest Showman (2017)
programme.desc: After losing his job as a bank clerk, PT Barnum creates and develops his circus in New York in the mid-1800s with the help of playwright Phillip Carlyle and an adopted family of entertainers shunned by society. Musical drama, starring Hugh Jackman, Zac Efron, Michelle Williams, Rebecca Ferguson and Zendaya

programme.title: Raging Bull (1980)
programme.desc: Biopic of boxer Jake LaMotta, who rose to fame as the world middleweight champion in the 1940s. His career was marred by an inability to deal with his anger, leaving his family bearing the brunt of his rage and paranoia. Directed by Martin Scorsese and starring an Oscar-winning Robert De Niro alongside Joe Pesci and Cathy Moriarty

programme.title: Serena (2014)
programme.desc: A couple move to North Carolina during the Great Depression to set up a logging empire. While their business proves successful, various revelations create tension between them and ruthless ambition drives both to violent and destructive ends. Period drama based on the novel by Ron Rash, starring Bradley Cooper, Jennifer Lawrence and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley looks forward to meeting Philip's sister when she visits from England, but her excitement vanishes when it becomes clear his sibling is unimpressed with his taste in girlfriends. Meanwhile, Danny's contract negotiations with the Rangers are put in jeopardy. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon develops a mental block and has difficulty working on a formula, so in a move inspired by Albert Einstein he gets a job at the Cheesecake Factory diner to try to revive his creativity - and despite Penny's objections, proves surprisingly effective in the role. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray is honoured as one of the area's top businessmen, Beverly's attempt to help her husband find a hobby backfires, and Erica comes to the rescue when Barry's scheme to steal golf carts goes awry

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: A scary TV movie motivates Barry to beg his father to build an underground bunker for the family, but Murray isn't easily persuaded. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Peralta notices Holt is in a bad mood, he teams up with the captain's husband Kevin to find out what has gone wrong. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Amy and Wil Wheaton get into a fight while filming Fun with Flags, Sheldon suffers the consequences of their fall-out. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends fight over a ring they believe was used in the Lord of the Rings film trilogy. Leonard hopes to return the item to its rightful owner - but Raj wishes to sell it so he can buy a jet ski, and Sheldon develops a Gollum-like obsession with the precious object

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As the academic year comes to a close, Barry and Erica wonder how they will appear in the school yearbook and pressure Adam, who is on the team producing it, to see the rough layout before it goes to print. Murray is annoyed when Marvin pays a visit to ask for money. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs

Barry's plans for a summer with Lainey turn to dust, while Dana makes an unexpected visit. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: As the wedding of Gina's mother and Boyle's father arrives, each member of the squad is given a task to complete for the big day. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's seldom-seen father Roger arrives to spend some time with his son, but Charles is sceptical of his true motives. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends try to solve the mystery of Sheldon's post-lunch activities, following him to a secret room where they discover how he chills out. Elsewhere, Leonard is jealous when Penny starts texting a classmate, but things quickly change when Alex gets flirty with him

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon feels undermined when his parking space at the university is allocated to Howard instead, and his determination to cling on to the spot causes a rift in the pair's friendship. However, the battle between the boys pales in comparison to that waged by Amy and Bernadette, whose disagreement on behalf of their significant others becomes increasingly personal. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Adam and Barry decide to write a song like the iconic Parents Just Don't Understand. Beverly keeps calling Erica at college, but Erica wants some space from her mother and screens her phone calls, forcing Beverly to adopt drastic measure

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Erica tells Beverly she is going to spend the first night of Hanukkah with Geoff and his family, Beverly starts a competition over who hosts the best Hanukkah party. Meanwhile, Barry seeks advice from his grandfather about how to be cool and Pops suggests that he should enter the school's holiday talent show

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While the girls go to Disneyland and get made up as princesses, the guys have to promote science as a career option for women at Howard's old middle school. Meanwhile, Raj and Lucy have their first date in a library, where instead of speaking to each other they send text messages

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Bernadette and Howard host a dinner party, but as it falls on Sheldon's Thai food night, he opts to bring his own dinner. As punishment for his lack of manners, the gang decides he should organise Wolowitz's cluttered wardrobe, but he discovers an unopened letter from Howard's father, who hasn't made contact in years

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Sienna feels like she has hit rock bottom, and Felix is torn between Martine and Grace. Meanwhile, Imran tries to support Brooke by turning up in the village wearing a tutu

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Meanwhile, Will takes a shine to new girl Lauren - but she is only interested in Simon. Comedy, starring Simon Bird

programme.title: The Inbetweeners

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon uses his holidays to help Amy in her biology lab, but moans about the menial jobs he is given - until she asks him to dissect a brain. Penny grows curious about Leonard's attitude to marriage when Bernadette asks Howard to sign a pre-nuptial agreement

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: When Rainbow makes a new friend at work who is having problems with her ex-husband, Dre realises that his wife has 'broken bird syndrome'. Andre Jr transforms his shared room with Jack into a frat house, so Ruby and Diane gang up against them

Meanwhile, Riley is not happy that Danny is going out with the sister of her ex-boyfriend. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley decides to travel by train to her family reunion in Florida, so Ben volunteers to tag along, seeing it as the perfect opportunity for them to rekindle their romance. When Tucker gets the chance to co-host a television show, he asks Bonnie for help with his audition tape. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is awarded a prestigious prize by the university, but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows he'll have to give a speech. Penny tries to gee him up with rousing references to X-Men, and Leonard and Raj offer psychological guidance - without much success

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny he loves her, but fails to get the response he would have liked. Sheldon also finds himself competing against Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Wil Wheaton once again when the gang goes bowling - and tries to get his revenge

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly and Murray help Erica move into her college dormitory, where her room-mate is also named Erica, but Beverly has a hard time saying goodbye to her daughter. Now that Lainey has gone to college, Adam tries to find the perfect girlfriend for Barry, even pretending to build one, as in the film Weird Science

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam has to tackle a school project on the Second World War, so decides to make a film featuring his grandfather. However, Pops takes matters into his own hands when Adam finds all his war stories mundane. Erica fails to cope with her own cooking and laundry at college and returns home to confront Beverly for over-mothering her, but Murray insists that Beverly starts to teach Erica and Barry basic life skills

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Santiago and Diaz investigate Jake's claim that somebody is trying to sabotage his professional life and get him dismissed. Boyle partners up with Hitchcock and Scull to solve a tough case, while Jeffords lies to Gina claiming he and Holt attended her dance troupe's performance. Chris Parnell guest stars

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake badly injures himself while chasing down a suspect, but then refuses to take time off from work to recover. Holt and Rosa have an awkward dinner date with their significant others, so they invite Amy and Gina along as well. Andy Samberg stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: As part of the relationship agreement, Sheldon reluctantly has to take care of Amy when she gets sick, but she loves the extra attention so much she pretends to remain in poor health. Elsewhere, Howard tries to get some tips on manliness from Penny when he and Bernadette go to visit her father

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tries to get Sheldon into the Christmas mood by playing a game, but things do not go as planned when Sheldon reveals a childhood trauma. Raj goes out with the girls and reveals he had crushes on both Penny and Bernadette - but not Amy. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon misses an opportunity to meet Spider-Man co-creator Stan Lee when he has to go to court for driving through a red light, and his anger leads to his imprisonment. Penny finds out the address of the comic-book writer and takes Sheldon there - but his troubles with the law have yet to reach their conclusion and the visit does not go as expected

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Afraid that she'll be left alone in her old age, Beverly seeks her children's advice on assembling a circle of friends for her later years and attempts to bond with other mothers to form their own group like The Golden Girls. Geoff's romantic gestures begin to annoy Erica, but when she tries to reciprocate, she almost brings their relationship to an end

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica is appalled when Geoff arranges for them to go out to dinner with her family to celebrate her birthday. Geoff soon discovers that dinner with the Goldbergs is no ordinary meal out: the family repeatedly change tables, Murray insists Adam order from the children's menu, the food is sent back to the kitchen, and Barry wants to amend his order but Beverly won't allow it. By the end of the evening, Geoff reaches breaking-point

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Charles take Terry on a camping trip to help him deal with stress. Amy gets Gina to help her persuade the police department to buy a product she invented as a child. Holt helps Rosa to break up with Marcus. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy work together on a case, but wind up arguing over Jake's old, worn-out mattress. Meanwhile, Boyle scratches Holt's prized car, and the teenage boy Rosa sponsors as a 'big brother' is in trouble with the law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Six weeks after the experiment ended, all the participants reunite for the first time at a final explosive dinner party

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys compete for a tenured professor position at the university, leading them to try to win over the committee in charge of appointing a new staff member, and arguing about who deserves the spot the most. However, the panel includes Mrs Davis from Human Resources, whom Sheldon has crossed paths with before. Guest starring Regina King

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy tries to help Sheldon get over his desire for closure when one of his favourite TV shows is cancelled on a cliffhanger ending. Raj discovers his new girlfriend Lucy has a blog in which she has written about him, and is upset when he learns she believes he has feminine qualities, causing him to worry about their relationship

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Martine and Grace both attend hospital appointments about lumps found in their breasts, before each of them have their interviews for the Businesswoman of the Year Award

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: Sandy's patience is tested when Hannah disobeys a direct order, and Joao feels betrayed when he overhears Kasey warning Brooke about his flirtatious ways

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is driven to distraction when a tune becomes stuck in his head, while Amy and Dave arrange another date and Howard and Raj discover their band has a fan, but tracking him down turns out to be a mistake. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik, Simon Helberg and Kunal Nayyar

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Another chance to see the special Stand Up to Cancer edition of the TV review series, originally shown during the night of fundraising in October 2018, with Louis Tomlinson and Liam Payne, Danny and Dani Dyer, Dele Alli and Eric Dier, and Peter Crouch and Abbey Clancy taking to their sofas. What do they think of The Haunting of Hill House, The X Factor, University Challenge, The Yorkshire Vet, Naked Attraction and more?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Glyn, a 60-year-old disco-dancing entertainer from Essex, hopes to find the perfect partner with whom he can dance off into the sunset. Twenty-eight-year-old Jamie has been on lots of dates, but she keeps meeting cheaters who leave her feeling cold, and needs the show's help to find an honest guy. Presented by Anna Richardson

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon refuses to get his hair cut as his regular barber is in a coma - a solitary change that causes chaos in the rest of his well-ordered life. Meanwhile, Howard undergoes a gruelling Nasa training programme in Houston, Texas, meaning he can only communicate with Bernadette via the internet

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is disappointed with Sheldon when he announces he is breaking his promise to go with her to a birthday party in favour of joining the boys for a Star Wars video-game marathon. Comedy, starring Mayim Bialik and Jim Parsons

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Capturing the households' instant reactions to programmes including The X Factor, This Time Next Year, Peter York's Hipster Handbook, A Nightmare on Elm Street, First Dates, Poldark and the News. First aired in November 2016

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alice Levine, Asim Chaudhry, Liza Tarbuck, Russell Howard and Tim Vine perform wheelbarrow stunts, build with fruit and emerge from wardrobes, as they compete for the ultimate prize - Taskmaster Greg Davies's disembodied head dipped in gold. As always, Alex Horne is on hand to assist with quiet dignity

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Glyn, a 60-year-old disco-dancing entertainer from Essex, hopes to find the perfect partner with whom he can dance off into the sunset. Twenty-eight-year-old Jamie has been on lots of dates, but she keeps meeting cheaters who leave her feeling cold, and needs the show's help to find an honest guy. Presented by Anna Richardson

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: In a first for the restaurant, mum and daughter Maria and Charlotte arrive for a double date. Maria meets Steve, a divorced 46-year-old builder who has a saucy way with the ladies, while Charlotte is a single mum looking for a man to heal her broken heart. Thirty-year-old academic Dan is matched with cancer cell biologist Jemma from north London, and other diners include long-distance lorry driver Paul who meets Brummie rocker Rozie

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon invites a famous physicist to the flat after befriending her online, but is put out to find Leonard showing an interest in her. Meanwhile, Penny has to confront her ex-boyfriend when the visitor turns up in his bedroom. Guest starring Judy Greer (Glenn Martin, DDS)

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre decides to put Andre Jr to work as an intern at Stevens & Lido during his gap year, while Rainbow allows Ruby to look after Devante when the nanny quits

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When Rainbow makes a new friend at work who is having problems with her ex-husband, Dre realises that his wife has 'broken bird syndrome'. Andre Jr transforms his shared room with Jack into a frat house, so Ruby and Diane gang up against them

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie sells the table football game to a student fraternity as part of her plan to 'baby proof' the apartment, unaware that Ben was using it as a hiding place for Emma's college tuition money. Meanwhile, Riley is not happy that Danny is going out with the sister of her ex-boyfriend. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Devon County Show, where she discovers the secret of home-made handscrub and learns about beekeeping and making beeswax candles. She also tries her hand at crafting jewellery in silver clay - an ideal way of making unusual but inexpensive gifts

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Martin and Michelle MacClay from Wiltshire have long dreamed of having a holiday home in France to escape from their busy lives along with their 13-year-old daughter Caitlin. They have chosen the Loire Valley as their perfect location because of its landscapes, rivers, historic chateaux and wonderful food. Scarlette Douglas sets out to help them find a property on a budget of £73,000.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole makes good money restoring an antique pine sideboard in Sheffield, but has less success with a pair of old brass jugs, while Simon converts a staircase into shelves

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Hampshire and Surrey, where Henry makes a pair of coffee tables from a cable drum and a galvanised water tank, and Simon rescues a winged back chair and upholsters it with his own designer fabric

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers look for the heirs of a man whose family worked for London Underground during the Blitz. Plus, the programme explores how investigations into an unclaimed estate revealed the story of an aviation engineer who helped design the Lysander plane before the Second World War

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to West Sussex to meet Med and Chandni, who are aiming to build a sizeable four-bed home on a minuscule budget by doing most of the work themselves. Med's never self-built before and he soon discovers that determination and blind optimism will only get you so far. As the project spirals out of control, can this couple really deliver the stylish home they crave?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham's modern Park Hotel in Gloucestershire, where sisters Sue Lewis and Wendy Germann are impressed by the colourful decor, but it's not an opinion held by all. The guests are not impressed with having to pay extra for breakfast and Callum and Lauren are faced with robust feedback that leaves them wanting answers and thinking not everyone is being fair

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to Sue's B&B, which is run by Sue Lewis and her sister, Wendy Germann. Breakfast at the communal table has its awkward moments and Sue is under pressure, having never cooked for so many before. However, it's the feedback that leaves the hosts wondering if maybe some of the guests have not been that straightforward

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Talbot in Iwerne Minster, Dorset for the third day of the competition, where hosts Erika Watt and Ellie Gill greet their guests. The rooms do not live up to their good first impression and Callum and Lauren are quick to find fault. At breakfast, tensions simmer as Wendy calls Cameron a creep, and at feedback, some of the comments send Erika and Ellie into fits of giggles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit of the week is to The Old Rectory in Llangattock, south Wales, where young hotel manager Cameron Thomas likes to keep things professional. Despite wanting to find fault, Lauren and Callum are soon to admit their bridal suite is top notch, but Erika and Ellie think there may be some cleaning issues. During an afternoon of canoe racing the group members show their competitive sides

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Park Hotel in Gloucestershire is up first as owners Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham want to get to the bottom of why Cameron didn't sleep soundly. Next, Sue Lewis from Sue's B&B seeks to discover the deasons for Callum and Lauren's low score for the cleanliness. When all issues are resolved, the winning establishment is revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the behaviour of the animals at Chester Zoo. Kendari the babirusa - a bizarre-looking creature also known as a deer-pig - is trying to give birth, but is being disturbed by a couple of mischievous otters. Young male orang-utan Willie struggles to impress the females, while Raz the aye-aye, a rare nocturnal primate related to lemurs, is being encouraged to share his home with two jumping rats

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests. With the first guests of the season about to turn up, the pool continues to be a problematic nightmare for Mandy and Steve. Dick's wife Angel shows some clever, cheap, wedding tips - but will Dick and the kids like them?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Jo and Shaun Bennett, a couple with a love of gothic architecture and horror movies, plan to use their budget of £400,000 to build an Addams Family-style house, complete with a stained-glass window, a stone fireplace and an oak staircase featuring gargoyles. However, completing the five-bedroom home on limited funds proves difficult

programme.title: Guy Martin's WWI Tank
programme.desc: The presenter builds a working replica of a First World War tank to mark the centenary of the Battle of Cambrai, and takes his tank driving test so he can take his creation on to the streets for Lincoln's Remembrance Day parade. He also delves into the history of the earliest armoured vehicles, the soldiers who crewed them, and the women who built them

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as guest captain Jason Manford and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Lee Mack and Fay Ripley. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley takes care of the letters and numbers

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Tom is rushed to St George's after crashing his bike head first into a parked car while racing a friend. Doctors order a CT scan to assess his injuries and must decide whether he will need emergency surgery to his face. Plus, 26-year-old Andy comes to A&E after injuring his knee during a game of Frisbee, and 51-year-old Jason arrives after suffering from blurred vision for two days

programme.title: Guy Martin's WWI Tank
programme.desc: The presenter builds a working replica of a First World War tank to mark the centenary of the Battle of Cambrai, and takes his tank driving test so he can take his creation on to the streets for Lincoln's Remembrance Day parade. He also delves into the history of the earliest armoured vehicles, the soldiers who crewed them, and the women who built them

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as guest captain Jason Manford and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Lee Mack and Fay Ripley. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley takes care of the letters and numbers

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp plans to surprise her guests with two main courses - roast beef with mulled wine gravy, and masala cauliflower with coconut stuffing for the vegetarians

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Gary Fitzpatrick and Shahine Korimbocus want a holiday home in the tranquil Limousin area of France where they can enjoy an outdoor lifestyle with their 20-month-old twins Charlie and Catherine, and Gary's son Dominic. Brought up with French culture in Mauritius, Shahine wants the children to share her enjoyment of it, and to pick up the language. With a budget of £88,000, Scarlette Douglas sets out to help the family find their perfect house

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Wales, where Henry restores a Royal Enfield motorbike, converting it into a classic cafe racer. Simon's choice of an old school house radiator springs a leak, but he gets an 1980s pinball machine back to its best

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate officers search for the heirs of an unclaimed estate, and reveal the story of a woman who left her native Ireland in the 1950s and was not heard of again until she died leaving a legacy of £1.7million. Plus, more details of unclaimed assets

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton catches up with a Leicestershire couple previously featured on the show to see how their plan to build a four-bedroom place in their garden has progressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Shiralee Bed & Breakfast in Salisbury, run by Fay Lahiani and her partner Ray Cooper, where some guests are shocked by the colourful and cluttered decor, while the presence of CCTV cameras also causes concern

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Bluebell Woods in Northampton, David and Donna Bletsoe Brown try to bring a touch of class to their woodland B&B, but Fay Lahiani and Ray Cooper are on the warpath and are quick to discover a mucky mattress. Meanwhile, Marc Harris and Wayne Williams reveal their competitive streak during an afternoon of horse riding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Lion Hotel in Pontypool for the third day of the contest, where manager Marc Harris and head chef Wayne Williams aim to deliver a high end service. Although some of the guests are impressed by the rooms, Ray and Fay find it too small for the price, and simmering tensions between some of the competitors leads to a clash over dinner. At breakfast, Wayne's food comes under fire, but it is David and Donna's feedback about finding a nail in their room that really rattles their hosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is the New Inn, Talgarth, where ex-naval officer Damon Lewis is the sole captain of his ship. Wayne and Marc are determined to be brutally honest, and Donna and David are forced to work closely with their rivals Fay and Ray during an afternoon team-building exercise. Over dinner, Damon admits that taking on the B&B, pub and restaurant all by himself may have not been the best decision - as proved when he has trouble running a solo breakfast service

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, with Fay and Ray from Shiralee B&B wanting to find out why David and Donna were so critical of their cleanliness. The group members continue to air their grievances as they find out what they've been paid, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the behaviour of the animals at Chester Zoo. An outbreak of disease in the penguin colony leaves chick Munch fighting for his life. Sulawesi macaque Lisa gives birth and dad Mamassa is providing pretty useless, so older brother Beaker steps in to help with the babysitting. Andean spectacled bear Bernie is determined to woo Lima when she enters her short window of fertility, and young male tortoise Football muscles in on 71-year-old Bert's harem of females

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge helps Donna McDougal, who believes a hoard of ancient armour was hidden in her chateau's lake. New parents in the Dordogne juggle the responsibility of looking after their baby with converting an attic into a bed-and-breakfast suite, and Limoges chateau owners Colette and Paul launch an ambitious new menu

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Pru and Richard Irvine decide to build a family home on an old industrial site in the Midlothian countryside, but will only be allowed to go ahead if their house blends in with the landscape. Unfortunately, the modern property they envisage could be considered an eyesore by the conservative locals and they underestimate the long hours needed to manage the project. Kevin McCloud monitors their progress

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Master mechanic Fuzz Townshend and petrolhead Tim Shaw return to rescue more classic cars for deserving owners. They begin in east London where they are confronted by a rusty old Porsche 356, which they claim to be the worst car they have ever taken on

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary charting the step-by-step process of the construction of impressive vehicles, beginning by following the making of a new £170,000 Continental GT in the Bentley factory. From the design teams to the craftsmen in the wood shop, this is the definitive guide of how to build a motor car

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-five-year-old Gerry is rushed to A&E with a dangerously high heart rate of more than 250bpm, and doctors suspect an underlying chest infection is the cause. Meanwhile, 92-year-old Margaret arrives at St George's after falling over and hitting her head, and six-year-old Daniel is treated for an infected blister on his foot

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with captain Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson taking on guest captain Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Master mechanic Fuzz Townshend and petrolhead Tim Shaw return to rescue more classic cars for deserving owners. They begin in east London where they are confronted by a rusty old Porsche 356, which they claim to be the worst car they have ever taken on

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary charting the step-by-step process of the construction of impressive vehicles, beginning by following the making of a new £170,000 Continental GT in the Bentley factory. From the design teams to the craftsmen in the wood shop, this is the definitive guide of how to build a motor car

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton is in Darwin in Australia's Northern Territory to find out how crocodiles are farmed for their meat, while Jimmy Doherty travels to Grimsby and Iceland as he investigates whether It's possible to make oil from salmon or haddock livers instead of the more traditional cod. Plus, Matt Tebbutt examines why bagels taste so different to bread despite the ingredients being virtually the same

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Francophiles Madeline and Andy Morton from Portsmouth are looking for a holiday home they can fly or drive to in the rural landscapes of the Charente region in west-central France. With cash savings of £45,000, they're hoping for a traditional two-bedroom home with charm and character. Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties, hoping that one will be their dream house

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items languishing in people's homes and use creative thinking and elbow grease to fix them up to turn a profit for their owners. Here they unearth a church pew, create a fish tank from scrap and uncover a special petrol tank

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in East Sussex and Hertfordshire, with finds that include a Victorian egg incubator

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers struggle with an estate that involves a series of wills dating back to the 19th century, and when a vicar dies in Surrey, the team returns to Middlesbrough to delve into its steelmaking history. Plus, details of unclaimed estates for which heirs still need to be found

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: A Cambridgeshire couple plan to build a four-bedroom detached house for the cost of a tiny terrace - which requires working around the clock and calling in a lot of favours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Sumatran tiger Kirana faces losing her two cubs, who are fully grown and being moved to other zoos to start families of their own. Indonesian dwarf buffalo Oana, one of Chester's most sensitive animals, gives birth - only for the new mum's anxiety to threaten the calf's health

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Donna McDougal's search for the armour in the lake continues. Tim and Krys, with the help of Tim's brother Ben, continue in their struggle to get the attic converted in time for the first guests of the season. Dick and his mother-in-law Jenny struggle with preparing and freezing 227kg of pork, before attempting to turn the final 2kg into salamis

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tiffany and Jonny Wood build a modernist home in Bath, opting for an environmentally friendly prefabricated German kit house, but first they have to prepare the site - an extremely steep hill. When the couple are close to starting construction, disaster strikes as a storm hits

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Matt reveals his plan for increasing biodiversity in the woodland and planting the apple tree orchard that his parents Mike and Janice have always dreamed of. Farrier John pays the donkeys a visit and is surprised to find them living next to an unexpected new neighbour, and Matt visits the garage to see whether they've had any luck getting dad his dad's old Dodge up and running

programme.desc: Singers on Buzzers and Shrewd Operators go head to head as they face tough questions on topics including science, sport and history. Hosted by Jon Snow

Meanwhile, 68-year-old Pat awaits the results of an X-ray after injuring her leg on holiday, and 89-year-old Adrian complains of chest pain

Farrier John pays the donkeys a visit and is surprised to find them living next to an unexpected new neighbour, and Matt visits the garage to see whether they've had any luck getting dad his dad's old Dodge up and running

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits one of Kenya's largest tea plantations and discovers that tea comes in multiple grades and flavours, and every batch can be subtly different. Matt Tebbutt dives in Cornwall for the edible seaweed that has started cropping up on supermarket shelves and Kate Quilton heads to Slovenia and Northern Ireland, eager to learn about the increasing use of rosemary in foodstuffs

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Late Nite Eats
programme.desc: Chef Jordan Andino is on the hunt to find restaurants that reign supreme in the late-night food scene, meeting other chefs to taste their most popular dishes

programme.title: Late Nite Eats
programme.desc: Jordan Andino visits Tucson, where chef Ramiro spends the day roasting a whole pig. His late night best seller is the Pasco Nachos, loaded with cheesy sauce and juicy pork

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank discover a huge and rare collection with enough items to fill a museum, but struggle to agree on a price for a set that is essentially priceless

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori take one of the biggest gambles of their lives on a mystery unit

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Former boxer Brandon Bernier and his wife Lori are anxious to see what is inside a locked coffin, but a rival thwarts their plans

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank continue their search for abandoned treasures. But when they bump into hard negotiator Big Moe, Mike is left feeling very small

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank attempt to unearth a rare vintage car, which could be worth a fortune

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Rival teams of amateur racers pit their wacky, gravity-powered soapbox vehicles in a downhill race against the clock in Paris

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compete in a 1950s-style race from London to Edinburgh. On the footplate of the A1 Tornado steam train, Jeremy has to feed the boiler with a constant supply of coal, while Richard braves the saddle of a Vincent Black Shadow motorbike and James drives an old Jaguar XK120 as the trio discover what Top Gear might have been like 60 years ago. Michael Schumacher drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond, Jeremy Clarkson and James May try to find the perfect car for 17-year-olds, dealing with insurance companies, accident repairs and a muddy music festival car park in the process. Richard also visits Abu Dhabi to test the Lamborghini Murcielago SV and stages a race between the 1000hp Bugatti Veyron and the McLaren F1. Actor, writer and comedian Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters find three small cars at an affordable price - a task that involves pestering bankers, harnessing large dogs and almost getting arrested. James May meets American rally driver Ken Block, and Jeremy Clarkson tests out the Mercedes SL Black. Comedian Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mail's delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Sally Phillips locks herself in the caravan with an amorous water cooler, and Aisling Bea cramsan Italian entree dish into a balloon. Alex Horne risks the wrath of his fierce employer Greg Davies with a cheeky unsanctioned task

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenter Claudia Winkleman, broadcaster Clive Anderson, comedian Jason Manford and comedienne Miranda Hart join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the comedy panel show, which sees veteran broadcaster Terry Wogan, cricketer-turned-TV personality Phil Tufnell and writer, presenter and poker player Victoria Coren Mitchell compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. Their gripes include food packaging, health fads, the 1990s Australian cricket team, hors d'oeuvres and people who walk around changing rooms naked

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner chairs the comedy panel show, inviting comedian Greg Davies, presenter and adventurer Ben Fogle and journalist Janet Street-Porter to compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. Their gripes include people who use suitcases on wheels, multichannel TV, umbrellas, friends' hobbies and women who like to be treated as ladies

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig talks garbage as she asks Alan Davies and this week's guest panellists, Bridget Christie, Johnny Vegas and Holly Walsh, to reveal all they know about rubbish

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to comics including Mel Giedroyc and Noel Fielding, with Alex Horne and Noel Fielding dancing like a pair of shrews

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy show, with Ellie Taylor, Josh Widdicombe, Rob Beckett and James Acaster joining regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons to give their irreverent takes on the biggest stories to hit the headlines hit the headlines in October 2015, through stand-up spots and improvised games. Chaired by Dara O Briain

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenter Claudia Winkleman, broadcaster Clive Anderson, comedian Jason Manford and comedienne Miranda Hart join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: TV doctor Christian Jessen, actor and presenter Warwick Davis and writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell tell Frank Skinner what really gets on their wick, in the hope he will banish their bugbears to the infamous room for ever. The celebrities' gripes include doctors, James Bond and people who send e-mails

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Mrs Brown star Brendan O'Carroll, comedian Bob Mortimer and Countdown numbers expert Rachel Riley tell Frank Skinner what really gets on their wick, in the hope he will banish their bugbears to the infamous room for ever. programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian reveals a story of invasion in Britain spanning millennia, beginning with him felling a tree with a flint axe in Kent, and riding an Iron Age chariot. Sam Willis also searches for clues of invasion at Silbury Hill, and tracks down evidence of the Beaker people, who brought ceramics, metalwork and beakers to Britain

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: Sam Willis tells the story of the Barbary Corsaire pirates who made their headquarters on Lundy Island. He also sheds light on the little-known tale of King Louis the Lion, a French King who invaded Britain in the 13th century

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Herbert Rowse Armstrong, who was convicted of murdering his wife with arsenic. Much of the evidence against him was circumstantial and the scientific testimony remains disputed. This episode examines both sides of the case and asks whether the British justice system made a terrible mistake

programme.title: Titanic: Stories from the Deep
programme.desc: The mysterious story of Howard Irwin, whose suitcase was aboard the Titanic, despite the fact that he wasn't, and the story behind the schoolbooks of Edgar Samuel Andrew and the chilling statement he wrote in a letter to his friend

programme.title: Titanic: Stories from the Deep
programme.desc: Thanks to the use of cutting-edge technology, a unique collection of artefacts salvaged from the underwater resting site of the famous wreck suggest new stories of love, deception, fate and heroics. This first episode touches on Fifth Officer Harold Lowe. After Titanic sank, his lifeboat was one of the few to go back and pick up survivors. One of the people he saved, Renee Harris, gifted him a set of gold-plated nautical equipment as a symbol of her gratitude. Plus, a well-preserved alligator bag recovered from the wreck site containing fascinating clues about the life of its owner

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the steel city of Sheffield where he discovers an early 20th century steam engine which produced armour plates for a Royal Navy warship. Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales. Along the way he discovers a childhood favourite - a miniature tank engine with three carriages in dark red - made by Frank Hornby, and learns the story of a Welsh countess who lived in Gwrych Castle. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Arley Hall and Gardens in Cheshire, where a time capsule that has been buried for 130 years is opened for the first time on camera. Other finds brought in include a gold bracelet with a tragic past that was found bricked up behind a wall, and a lost work by 19th-century artist Lawrence Alma-Tadema

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores Wingfield, one of the oldest surviving railways and uncovers the longest brick viaduct in the UK, the Welland Viaduct in Northamptonshire

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A closer look at a sprawling Wild West ghost town that sits peaceful and abandoned in Montana, USA, but is still haunted by tales of murder, corruption and buried treasure

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A vast project in the icy wilderness of southern Alaska, and a landscape of confusing and surprising structures that represented a bold statement of Spain's plans for the future

programme.title: World War Two From Above
programme.desc: For the first time, the theatre of war takes place mainly in the air. From the invention of radar to Hitler's 'Wonder Weapons', all means are good to bring the enemy down

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Major General Robert Laycock who rallied his ill-equipped troops to see off a German attack on Crete, Greece

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys

programme.title: The Good Nazi
programme.desc: The story of Major Karl Plagge, a high-ranking Nazi officer who risked his own life to save hundreds of people, in a story reminiscent of Schindler's List

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story of America's ‘Liberty Ships', which were produced in incredible numbers and became known as the Model T Fords of the ocean

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Chronicling the events that brought about the demise of the Third Reich, as the Allies arrived at the gates of Berlin and Hitler boarded himself up in his bunker, where it is believed he committed suicide. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Following a break-in, Arkwright decides to buy a guard dog to keep away potential burglars. Unfortunately, his new investment also scares off the customers

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Miserly shopkeeper Arkwright organises a sales drive in a bid to boost profits - and rid himself of the vast consignment of apples he has mistakenly ordered. An unhappy Granville is persuaded to demonstrate the merchandise. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's washing-machine breaks down, and Arkwright decides to try and lure her into his home with the offer of his vintage machine

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the train operating company's drivers, managers, ticket inspectors and technicians along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. In the first edition, Paddington's duty station manager Simon Jeffery has his customer service skills tested by several angry passengers who have been held up following a fire, while train manager Jodie deals with drunks on a late-night service from Swansea

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Staff have to deal with more than 100,000 festival-goers travelling to Glastonbury by rail from around Britain and the royal train stops at St Erth station in Cornwall, before the Queen and the Duke of Edinburgh board a scheduled service to St Ives. There is drama at Castle Cary when a passenger falls on to the tracks and police and workers struggle to pull him to safety

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian discovers French plans to invade Britain by balloon, the subterranean fortress built for troops in the 19th century when invasion paranoia was at fever pitch, and examines the rich legacy of the Huguenots

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Hampton Court Palace, which started out life as a Tudor palace in 1515 and is famous for being the home of King Henry VIII

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The crimes of Amelia Dyer, who promised mothers that for a fee she would adopt their babies and raise them as her own. In fact, she neglected and murdered countless infants over a 30-year period in Victorian Britain. This episode tells the story of Dyer and of Evelina Marmon, one of the desperate young mothers she deceived

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: The historical significance of artefacts for sale at auction, beginning with items associated with Queens Victoria and Elizabeth II and the German battleship Bismarck

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Artefacts for sale at auction with connections to William Shakespeare and a Nazi fighter pilot, as well as rare dinosaur skeletons and a tool used to design the city of Bath

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, beginning with London to Monte Carlo. His first stop is Paris, where he absorbs the atmosphere, before heading south to the Cote d'Azur and ending his journey at Monaco's gaming tables

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns to the gardens of the Arley Hall, Cheshire, where a selection of family treasures come under scrutiny. Among the items brought in to be valued are a portrait painted in India in the 1950s, which expert Amin Jaffer identifies as the work of an acclaimed artist whose paintings have become much sought after in recent years, while other finds include a PoW's diary written while building the bridge over the River Kwai and a collection of gems once worn by a duchess

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Chris Tarrant goes on three journeys by rail through some of the world's harshest terrain. He begins by heading to the Congo in central Africa to experience one of the greatest surviving railways of the colonial era, built by the French at a cost of tens of thousands of native lives and connecting the capital Brazzaville to the coast. Chris sets off on his scheduled two-day trip from the station at the port of Pointe-Noire, ending his journey six days - and a whole series of setbacks - later

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A ghostly house in a tropical paradise that is still feared by the locals and a pioneering complex marked by both glory and tragedy that will soon to be lost forever

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The desert town that faced a disaster of biblical proportions. Plus, scorched remains that speak of imprisonment and torture and vast coastal ruins that reveal a past once wiped from history

programme.title: World War Two From Above
programme.desc: Using cutting edge drone technology, experts revisit some of the key sites across the former Third Reich to understand how the Nazi war machine operated

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: How Field Marshall Erich von Manstein's escape from the Red Army influenced Nato's defence plans

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns to the gardens of the Arley Hall, Cheshire, where a selection of family treasures come under scrutiny. Among the items brought in to be valued are a portrait painted in India in the 1950s, which expert Amin Jaffer identifies as the work of an acclaimed artist whose paintings have become much sought after in recent years, while other finds include a PoW's diary written while building the bridge over the River Kwai and a collection of gems once worn by a duchess

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, Michael Portillo explores the former domain of the Habsburg monarchs, beginning in the Hungarian capital Budapest. He then travels to Austria via Bratislava in Slovakia, and in Vienna he immerses himself in pre-First World War decadence. From there he heads to Salzburg, before going on to the Salzkammergut region to visit the emperor's Austrian summer residence at Bad Ischl, where in 1914 Franz Joseph I signed a declaration that changed the course of European history

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A rare Triumph motorcycle is rescued from a barn and resurrected. There's a battle against the elements for vintage aircraft to grace Brooklands' skies once again

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree; Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Anglophile Derek struggles to get his message across on a rare trip abroad to pick up a Bentley, while a well-restored Ford XR2 sets the auction alight

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: A power cut on a sweltering summer night gives Victor and Margaret time to reflect on their lives together and look forward, without enthusiasm, to a visit from their elderly neighbours. Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Roy Hudd star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor is invited to talk about evolution at the Women's Institute, and finds plenty of inspiration when he decides to take up fishing. Meanwhile, Pippa is left shattered after a three-week visit by Patrick's equally stuffy brother. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: After years of frustrated anger and railing against the system, Victor Meldrew breathes his last 'I don't believe it' after falling victim to a hit-and-run driver. At first, it seems as if the shock of his death will take a long time to settle for long-suffering Margaret

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Staff cope with the pressure of rush hour during the summer, with Lorel Kinghorn trying to get a group of rowdy schoolchildren safely on board a train without causing major delays on the line. There is a chance to find out what happens during the routine weekly maintenance, and Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and Emma Taylor take to the air as part of their commitment to preserve some of the most historic parts of the railway

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Staff carry out their duties through the night, with Reading's duty station manager Gary Brownlie trying to stop students dodging train fares. Paul Eddolls works a typical late shift in Bristol, where sleepy passengers and drunken revellers make his job difficult, and a service disruption causes chaos for Dean Haynes at London's busy Ealing Broadway station

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: Trading History
